name: Japanese N3 Grammar
description: JLPT N3 grammar patterns — fill in the correct grammar point to complete each sentence

facts.quizQuestion:
食べ過ぎて、イチゴ{___}食べられない。
(I am so full I could not [even] eat a strawberry.)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — 食べ過ぎて、イチゴさえ食べられない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Being too full to eat even a strawberry is an extreme case — さえ highlights the most minimal thing as an example of impossibility.

facts.quizQuestion:
食べ物がなくなれば、人間はネズミ{___}食べる。
(If there is no food, humans would [even] eat a mouse.)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — 食べ物がなくなれば、人間はネズミさえ食べる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eating a mouse is the extreme thing humans might do when desperate — さえ marks it as the surprising outer limit of what's possible.

facts.quizQuestion:
ほとんどの人がその存在{___}知らないようなこと
(thing which most people don't [even] know exists)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — ほとんどの人がその存在さえ知らないようなこと
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Most people don't even know this thing exists — さえ marks existence itself as the minimum level of awareness that's being denied.

facts.quizQuestion:
ソースコードを見つけられ{___}しないのに、開発するなんてもってのほかだ。
(they can't [even] -find- the source code, let alone finish developing it!)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — ソースコードを見つけられさえしないのに、開発するなんてもってのほかだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: They can't even find the source code — さえ emphasizes that finding it, the bare minimum step, is beyond them.

facts.quizQuestion:
定規を使って{___}、まっすぐに線が引けない。
([Even] using a ruler, he cannot draw a straight line.)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — 定規を使ってさえ、まっすぐに線が引けない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Even with a ruler — the easiest aid available — straight lines are still impossible. さえ marks using a ruler as the minimal helpful thing.

facts.quizQuestion:
今日の会議でこの議題{___}議論をしましょう。
(Let's make this matter the [central topic] of the discussion at today's meeting,)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — 今日の会議でこの議題を中心に議論をしましょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This agenda item is placed at the center of the discussion — を中心に sets something as the focal point around which other things revolve.

facts.quizQuestion:
駅{___}たくさんの商店が集まっている。
([Centered around] the station there are many stores.)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — 駅を中心にたくさんの商店が集まっている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Stores have gathered around the station as their central hub — を中心に describes the station as the focal point of the surrounding commercial area.

facts.quizQuestion:
中国を海外展開計画の{___}議論しましょう
(Let's make China the [center] of our overseas expansion plan discussion)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — 中国を海外展開計画の中心に議論しましょう
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: China is the central focus of the overseas expansion plan — を中心に places China at the core of the discussion.

facts.quizQuestion:
この地方{___}として、山地は、本州の東北では三列に、同じく西南では二列に分かれて連なっています。
([Centered around] this region, three separate mountain ranges extend towards north-east of Honshu, and two separate mountain ranges extend to the south-western part of Honshu)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — この地方を中心として、山地は、本州の東北では三列に、同じく西南では二列に分かれて連なっています。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This region is the geographic center around which the mountain ranges extend — を中心 (followed by として) marks it as the axis of the spatial description.

facts.quizQuestion:
その家のことはすべてメアリーおばさん{___}動いていた。
(Everything in that house [revolved around] Aunt Mary.)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — その家のことはすべてメアリーおばさんを中心に動いていた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Aunt Mary was the central person around whom the whole household revolved — を中心に captures her role as the pivot of the family's daily life.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は日本に行った{___}日本語を勉強した
([When] I was in Japan, I studied Japanese.)
facts.explanation: 時 (when) — 私は日本に行った時日本語を勉強した
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Studying Japanese happened during a general time period in Japan — 時 is the natural choice for a broad 'when' without pinpointing an exact moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつでも都合のいい{___}にお金を返してください。
(Pay me back [when] it's convenient.)
facts.explanation: 時 (when) — いつでも都合のいい時にお金を返してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Pay back whenever the timing works for you — 時 refers to a general, flexible time occasion rather than a specific scheduled moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
この部屋は物置{___}使っている。
(I'm using this room [as] a storeroom.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — この部屋は物置として使っている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The room is being used in the role of a storeroom — として marks the capacity or function the room is assigned, not its inherent nature.

facts.quizQuestion:
李さんは自動車の会社でエンジニア{___}働いている。
(Mister Lee is working in a car maker firm [as] an engineer.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 李さんは自動車の会社でエンジニアとして働いている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Mr. Lee works in the role of an engineer — として shows the professional capacity in which he is employed at the company.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は単に冗談{___}それを言った。
(He said it merely [as] a joke.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 彼は単に冗談としてそれを言った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He made the comment in the capacity of a joke, not seriously — として frames the statement's intended role or purpose.

facts.quizQuestion:
先生{___}働いています。
(I work [as] a teacher.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 先生として働いています。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Working as a teacher describes the role the speaker fills — として identifies the professional capacity, not a personal quality.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は医者{___}あなたに言います。
(I'll tell you this [as] a doctor.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 私は医者としてあなたに言います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Speaking in the professional capacity of a doctor gives weight to the advice — として sets the role or standpoint from which the speaker addresses you.

facts.quizQuestion:
両親の期待{___}女の子が生まれた。
([Contrary to] the parents expectations, a girl was born.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 両親の期待に反して女の子が生まれた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A girl was born, going against what the parents had hoped for — に反して marks the outcome as directly contrary to an expectation or wish.

facts.quizQuestion:
国民の期待{___}政府は年金制度改正法案の強行採決に踏み切った。
([Contrary to] the peoples expectations, the government bulldozed the pension system revision bill through the Diet.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 国民の期待に反して政府は年金制度改正法案の強行採決に踏み切った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The government's action was the opposite of what people expected — に反して shows the action went directly against a stated expectation.

facts.quizQuestion:
研究社員になる希望{___}大学を止めらなければなりませんでした。
(I had to drop out of university [against] my aspirations of becoming a scientist.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 研究社員になる希望に反して大学を止めらなければなりませんでした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Dropping out went against the speaker's own aspiration of becoming a scientist — に反して shows the outcome opposed a personal goal.

facts.quizQuestion:
神の意向{___}人間は自然を破壊している。
([Contrary to] God's will, humans are destroying nature.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 神の意向に反して人間は自然を破壊している。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Destroying nature goes against God's will — に反して frames humans' behavior as directly in opposition to a stated will or intention.

facts.quizQuestion:
（僕の期待{___}）実のところ物価は毎週上昇している。
(([Against] my expectations) prices are going up every week.（M)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — （僕の期待に反して）実のところ物価は毎週上昇している。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Prices rising weekly is the opposite of what the speaker was hoping for — に反して marks the outcome as going contrary to a personal expectation.

facts.quizQuestion:
朝寝坊した。{___}仕事に遅れたんだ。
(I overslept. [Due to that], I was late for work.)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — 朝寝坊した。それで仕事に遅れたんだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Links the oversleeping to its direct consequence — the lateness happened as a natural follow-on result.

facts.quizQuestion:
３時のバスで行こうよ。{___}いいかい？
(I say we catch the 3 o'clock bus. [Is that alright] with you?)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — ３時のバスで行こうよ。それでいいかい？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Proposes a plan and then asks if that outcome works for the other person — checking whether the arrangement is acceptable.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}どうなりましたか？
([And then] what happened?)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — それでどうなりましたか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Prompts for the next step in a sequence — asking what came after the previous part of the story.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}このカードに記入してください。
([Then] you just have to fill out this card.)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — それでこのカードに記入してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Moves to the final step in a process — after everything else is handled, this is the remaining action.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}これ以上の義務を負わなくてよくなる。
([That] absolves me from further responsibility.)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — それでこれ以上の義務を負わなくてよくなる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: States that a specific result follows from a condition — completing that action is what makes the further obligation go away.

facts.quizQuestion:
このコンピューター１年前に買ったばかり{___}、もうすっかり時代遅れだわ。
([Even though] I bought this computer one year ago, it's already old fashioned.)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — このコンピューター１年前に買ったばかりなのに、もうすっかり時代遅れだわ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Bought only a year ago, yet already outdated — なのに expresses frustration at the gap between the expected and actual situation.

facts.quizQuestion:
この情報は当然最新であるべき{___}、実際はそうでない。
(This information is not as up-to-date [as it should be].)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — この情報は当然最新であるべきなのに、実際はそうでない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The information should be current but isn't — なのに highlights the surprising or unsatisfying contrast between what should be and what is.

facts.quizQuestion:
これは新しい靴{___}もう修理する必要がある。
(These new shoes [already] want mending.)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — これは新しい靴なのにもう修理する必要がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: New shoes needing repair is surprising and annoying — なのに conveys the speaker's frustration that reality doesn't match expectations.

facts.quizQuestion:
ずっと歩いて来たとは、あなたは足が丈夫{___}ちがいない。
(You must have a strong leg to have walked [all the way].)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — ずっと歩いて来たとは、あなたは足が丈夫なのにちがいない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Having walked all the way implies strong legs — なのに connects the evidence to the surprised conclusion in a logical-contrast chain.

facts.quizQuestion:
ジムは目覚めると、自分が部屋に１人{___}気がついた。
(Jim awoke and found himself [alone] in the room.)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — ジムは目覚めると、自分が部屋に１人なのに気がついた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Jim realized he was alone — なのに here introduces the surprising condition he found himself in upon waking.

facts.quizQuestion:
私のボスは、短気で怒り{___}。　　　（怒りっぽい is conventional.）
(My boss is short-tempered and [tends to] get angry easily.)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to) — 私のボスは、短気で怒りがち。　　　（怒りっぽい is conventional.）
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes a recurring negative pattern — the boss doesn't just get angry sometimes, it's a reliable habit.

facts.quizQuestion:
担当者の好き嫌いによる誤解も場合によっては起こり{___}。
(In some cases, misunderstanding [tends to] occur due to the likes and dislikes of the person in charge.)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to) — 担当者の好き嫌いによる誤解も場合によっては起こりがち。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Marks a likely recurring problem — misunderstandings due to personal bias are framed as something that happens more often than desired.

facts.quizQuestion:
曇り{___}
(mainly [cloudy])
facts.explanation: がち (tend to / prone to) — 曇りがち means weather that is predominantly cloudy. Attaches to noun or verb-stem to mark a recurring tendency.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes weather that leans heavily toward one condition — it's predominantly cloudy as a recurring state.

facts.quizQuestion:
遠慮{___}
([Tends to be] reserved/timid)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to / prone to) — 遠慮がち labels a habitual personality trait of holding back. Often pairs with negative or undesirable tendencies.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Labels a person's consistent behavioral pattern — holding back is their default, repeated tendency.

facts.quizQuestion:
お金持ちは貧乏人を見下し{___}である。
(The rich are [apt to] look down upon the poor.)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to) — お金持ちは貧乏人を見下しがちである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes a social tendency with a negative tone — the rich looking down on the poor is presented as a habitual pattern.

facts.quizQuestion:
全然見込みがないわけ{___}が、あまり期待はしない方がいいでしょう
(it [does not mean] that there is no hope, but you'd better not expect too much)
facts.explanation: 訳ではない (It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜) — 全然見込みがないわけではないが、あまり期待はしない方がいいでしょう
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: Softens the situation — it doesn't follow logically that there's zero hope, but you shouldn't count on things going well.

facts.quizQuestion:
あいつは馬鹿というわけ{___}がそれにしても、やってることはどうかなあ。
(While I am [not saying] he is stupid, I have my doubts when I look at what he is actually doing.)
facts.explanation: 訳ではない (It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜) — あいつは馬鹿というわけではないがそれにしても、やってることはどうかなあ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Pushes back without full accusation — the speaker isn't calling him stupid outright, but has serious doubts about his actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
世界中の人が同じ言語を話すわけ{___}ので、現在、翻訳に多くの努力が費やされている。
(Because people in the world [don't] speak the same language, much effort is now devoted to translation.)
facts.explanation: 訳ではない (It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜) — 世界中の人が同じ言語を話すわけではないので、現在、翻訳に多くの努力が費やされている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: States a logical reality — it's not the case that everyone speaks the same language, which is exactly why translation is needed.

facts.quizQuestion:
あの男しかいないわけ{___}よ。
(He is [not] the only pebble on the beach.)
facts.explanation: 訳ではない (It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜) — あの男しかいないわけではないよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Challenges an assumption — it doesn't necessarily follow that he's the only option; there are other people out there.

facts.quizQuestion:
あれは全く誤りというわけ{___}。
(That is [not altogether] false.)
facts.explanation: 訳ではない (It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜) — あれは全く誤りというわけではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Partially defends something — it doesn't follow that it's completely wrong, though it may not be fully correct either.

facts.quizQuestion:
毒魚ってなん{___}？
("Poison fish" - whadd'ya [suppose] they are?)
facts.explanation: だろう (is, would be, probably is; isn't it?) — 毒魚ってなんだろう？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Expresses wondering or guessing — the speaker is curious and musing about what 'poison fish' could possibly be.

facts.quizQuestion:
「洞窟の中で何が起こっているん{___}？すごく知りたいな。」「見当もつかないよ。」
("What's going on in the cave? I'm [curious]." "I have no idea.")
facts.explanation: だろう (is, would be, probably is; isn't it?) — 「洞窟の中で何が起こっているんだろう？すごく知りたいな。」「見当もつかないよ。」
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used inside a question to express genuine curiosity about something uncertain — the speaker really wants to know what's happening in the cave.

facts.quizQuestion:
「何時{___}か」と彼は疑った。
("What time [is it]?" he wondered.)
facts.explanation: だろう (is, would be, probably is; isn't it?) — 「何時だろうか」と彼は疑った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Works inside a reported thought — the character is internally guessing at something he doesn't know for sure.

facts.quizQuestion:
10年後には私たちの町も大いに変わる{___}。
(In ten years our town [will] change a lot.)
facts.explanation: だろう (is, would be, probably is; isn't it?) — 10年後には私たちの町も大いに変わるだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A confident prediction about the future — the speaker expects significant change in ten years, but it's still a guess.

facts.quizQuestion:
中野先生はたいてい研究室にいらっしゃるのに、今日{___}いらしゃらなかった。
(Professor Nakano is usually in his office, but today, [of all days], he wasn't there.)
facts.explanation: に限って (of all the ... / especially / only / just) — 中野先生はたいてい研究室にいらっしゃるのに、今日に限っていらしゃらなかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Captures the irony — of all days, the professor happened to be absent on the one day the speaker needed him.

facts.quizQuestion:
嫌なことがある時{___}、嫌なことが重なる。
([Only] when something unpleasant happens, do other unpleasant things occur.)
facts.explanation: に限って (of all the ... / especially / only / just) — 嫌なことがある時に限って、嫌なことが重なる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Highlights a frustrating pattern — it seems like only when something bad happens do more bad things pile on.

facts.quizQuestion:
うちの子供{___}そんなひどいことはしませんよ。
(our child, [out of all children], would not do such a terrible thing)
facts.explanation: に限って (of all the ... / especially / only / just) — うちの子供に限ってそんなひどいことはしませんよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Expresses confident defense — of all children, this speaker's own child would never do something so terrible.

facts.quizQuestion:
傘を持っていない日{___}、雨が降る。
(Just [on a day when] you don't have an umbrella with you, it will rain.)
facts.explanation: に限って (of all the ... / especially / only / just) — 傘を持っていない日に限って、雨が降る。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Classic Murphy's Law situation — out of all possible days, rain only comes on the exact day you forgot your umbrella.

facts.quizQuestion:
参加者は女性{___}る
(Female participants [only].)
facts.explanation: に限って (of all the ... / especially / only / just) — 参加者は女性に限る
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Restricts participation to one group — the event is exclusively for female participants, no others.

facts.quizQuestion:
ローマ字{___}書けません。
(I can not write anything [but] romaji [Roman letters]. (western person)
facts.explanation: しか (but, only, just) — ローマ字しか書けません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker can only write romaji and nothing else — しか pairs with the negative verb to express that limitation.

facts.quizQuestion:
田中さん{___}来ませんでした。
(No one came [except] Mr Tanaka.)
facts.explanation: しか (but, only, just) — 田中さんしか来ませんでした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Only Mr. Tanaka came — everyone else is excluded. しか + negative verb expresses that no one outside this one person showed up.

facts.quizQuestion:
百円{___}ない。
(I [only] have 100 yen)
facts.explanation: しか (but, only, just) — 百円しかない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Having only 100 yen is a limiting constraint — しか with ない conveys the smallness of what's available.

facts.quizQuestion:
赤いもの{___}ない。
([Nothing but] red things.)
facts.explanation: しか (but, only, just) — 赤いものしかない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Nothing but red things exist here — everything else is absent. しか with ない expresses total exclusion of other options.

facts.quizQuestion:
そこは車で{___}行けない。
(you can [only] go there by car)
facts.explanation: しか (but, only, just) — そこは車でしか行けない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The only way to get there is by car — no other method works. しか with the negative marks car as the sole route.

facts.quizQuestion:
ジョンは怒り{___}。
(John gets angry [easily].)
facts.explanation: っぽい (-like) — ジョンは怒りっぽい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Captures a personality characteristic — John being quick to anger is described as a defining trait, not just one incident.

facts.quizQuestion:
子ども{___}表現だという印象があります
(The expression has a [childish] ring to it)
facts.explanation: っぽい (-like) — 子どもっぽい表現だという印象があります
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Conveys that the expression has a childish quality or flavor — it gives off that impression rather than being definitively childish.

facts.quizQuestion:
かわいい{___}上原さん。
(Ms. Uehara, how [cute-ish] you look.)
facts.explanation: っぽい (-like) — かわいいっぽい上原さん。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes someone as having a cute vibe or look to them — it's a subjective impression rather than a hard fact.

facts.quizQuestion:
なに{___}って！
(What do you mean, [-ish]!)
facts.explanation: っぽい (-like) — なにっぽいって！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used to quote or repeat a vague characterization — the speaker is reacting to being called something with an -ish quality.

facts.quizQuestion:
ローラちゃんは色{___}よ。
(Laura is [sexy-like]!)
facts.explanation: っぽい (-like) — ローラちゃんは色っぽいよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes someone as giving off a sensual quality — it's the impression or aura they project, not a definitive label.

facts.quizQuestion:
「納得いかないんですよ」「何が？何がです{___}？」
("I just can't accept that!" "What? What is it that you can't accept[?]")
facts.explanation: の (? (sentence ending particle - question) [F]) — 「納得いかないんですよ」「何が？何がですの？」
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: One person is pushing back and the other asks a clarifying question. の here is a casual, slightly pointed question marker used in conversation.

facts.quizQuestion:
男・女：もうお店やっていない{___}？！まだ4時だよ。
(This shop is already closed, [is it]? It's still 4.)
facts.explanation: の (? (sentence ending particle - question) [F]) — 男・女：もうお店やっていないの？！まだ4時だよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is surprised the shop is already closed. の turns the statement into a casual, slightly incredulous question.

facts.quizQuestion:
なんで彼女がいない{___}？
(Why don't you have a girlfriend[?] [M/F])
facts.explanation: の (? (sentence ending particle - question) [F]) — なんで彼女がいないの？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A casual question asking why someone doesn't have a girlfriend. の makes it sound conversational and informal, not harsh.

facts.quizQuestion:
悪いけどほかに用事がある{___}。
(I'm sorry, I have another engagement.[F])
facts.explanation: の (? (sentence ending particle - question) [F]) — 悪いけどほかに用事があるの。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is declining with a casual explanation. の here softens the statement into an informal, conversational explanation-as-question.

facts.quizQuestion:
「どうした{___}。」「ほっといて。」
("What's wrong[?]" "Leave me alone.")
facts.explanation: の (? (sentence ending particle - question) [F]) — 「どうしたの。」「少しほうっておいて。あなたには関係ないことなの。」
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: One speaker asks what's wrong in a soft, concerned way. の makes the question feel personal and gentle rather than blunt.

facts.quizQuestion:
アメリカ大陸はコロンブス{___}発見された。
(The American continent was discovered [by] Columbus.)
facts.explanation: によって (by; according to; due to; owing to; due to) — アメリカ大陸はコロンブスによって発見された。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Columbus is the agent who performed the discovery — によって in passive sentences identifies who carried out the action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Dan Sman さん{___}書かれた本
(A book written [by] Mr Dan Sman)
facts.explanation: によって (by; according to; due to; owing to; due to) — Dan Sman さんによって書かれた本
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Mr. Dan Sman is the author who wrote the book — によって marks the agent responsible for creating or producing something.

facts.quizQuestion:
列車の代わりに飛行機に乗ること{___}失った時間を埋め合わすことができる。
(You can make up for lost time [by] taking an airplane instead of a train.)
facts.explanation: によって (by; according to; due to; owing to; due to) — 列車の代わりに飛行機に乗ることによって失った時間を埋め合わすことができる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Taking a plane is the method used to make up for lost time — によって marks the means or method by which the result is achieved.

facts.quizQuestion:
理想は言葉{___}表現される。
(Thoughts are expressed [by means of] words.)
facts.explanation: によって (by; according to; due to; owing to; due to) — 理想は言葉によって表現される。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Words are the means by which thoughts get expressed — によって marks the medium or tool through which something is communicated.

facts.quizQuestion:
リンゴは彼女{___}ナイフで半分に切られた。
(The apple was cut in two [by] her with a knife.)
facts.explanation: によって (by; according to; due to; owing to; due to) — リンゴは彼女によってナイフで半分に切られた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She is the agent who performed the cutting — によって identifies the person who carried out the passive action.

facts.quizQuestion:
今年は去年{___}、雨の量が多い。
(This year, [compared to] last year, the quantity of rain is much more.)
facts.explanation: に比べて (compared to) — 今年は去年に比べ、雨の量が多い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Sets last year as the reference point — this year's rainfall amount is being measured against what last year had.

facts.quizQuestion:
ノーパンクタイヤはエアタイヤ{___}寿命が長く、理想的です。
("no puncture" tires [in comparison with] air tires have a long useful life and are ideal)
facts.explanation: に比べて (compared to) — ノーパンクタイヤはエアタイヤに比べて寿命が長く、理想的です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Places air tires as the benchmark — puncture-free tires are evaluated by how they measure up against that standard.

facts.quizQuestion:
この作品は君の前の{___}進歩している。
(This work is an improvement [on] your last.)
facts.explanation: に比べて (compared to) — この作品は君の前のに比べて進歩している。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Holds the previous work as the baseline — progress is measured by how this piece stacks up against what came before.

facts.quizQuestion:
この本はあの本{___}、ためになるばかりでなくおもしろい。
(This book is not only more instructive but more interesting [than] that book.)
facts.explanation: に比べて (compared to) — この本はあの本に比べて、ためになるばかりでなくおもしろい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Uses the other book as the comparison point — this book is shown to be better in two ways when placed side by side with that one.

facts.quizQuestion:
それは以前{___}格段に進歩した。
(It has greatly improved [compared with] what it was.)
facts.explanation: に比べて (compared to) — それは以前に比べて格段に進歩した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Takes its earlier state as the reference — the improvement is described in terms of how different it is from what it used to be.

facts.quizQuestion:
店の前にいつも人が並んでいる{___}、人気のある店だということがわかる。
([Since] there's always a line of people in front of the store, I understand the fact that it's a popular shop.)
facts.explanation: ことから (because) — 店の前にいつも人が並んでいることから、人気のある店だということがわかる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Uses a visible, observable fact — the constant line of people — as the basis for a logical conclusion about the shop.

facts.quizQuestion:
町並みが昔のままに保存され、古い寺も多い{___}、その町は「小京都」と呼ばれている。
([Since] the streets are maintained to look how they did long ago, and since there are lots of old temples, that town is called "Little Kyoto.")
facts.explanation: ことから (because) — 町並みが昔のままに保存され、古い寺も多いことから、その町は「小京都」と呼ばれている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Points to multiple visible features of the town as the combined evidence behind its nickname.

facts.quizQuestion:
文字の{___}看板のことまでご相談下さい。
(Please feel free to contact us about anything [from] text to signboards.)
facts.explanation: ことから (because) — 文字のことから看板のことまでご相談下さい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Marks the starting point of a range — covering everything from the most basic element (text) all the way through to signboards.

facts.quizQuestion:
皆さん、話がおしゃむ君の{___}逸れてるんですけど｡
('Scuze me all, but the subject's about Oshamu-kun so you're getting [off topic].)
facts.explanation: ことから (because) — 皆さん、話がおしゃむ君のことから逸れてるんですけど｡
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Anchors the topic by naming its source — the discussion is supposed to be about Oshamu-kun, and that's where it started.

facts.quizQuestion:
そういうことだ{___}。
([Because] it's supposed to be like that.)
facts.explanation: ことから (because) — そういうことだから。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A simple spoken explanation — the speaker is giving the reason why something is the way it is.

facts.quizQuestion:
愛{___}が人を強くする
(It is love [that] truly strengthens people.)
facts.explanation: こそ (this, especially (emphasis),very, emphasize the preceding word) — 愛こそが人を強くする
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Love — not anything else — is specifically what strengthens people. こそ puts strong emphasis on love as the true source.

facts.quizQuestion:
今度{___}やってやる。
(I'm gonna do it [this time])
facts.explanation: こそ (this, especially (emphasis),very, emphasize the preceding word) — 今度こそやってやる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This time specifically is the moment the speaker is determined to succeed — こそ intensifies the resolve attached to 今度.

facts.quizQuestion:
夢{___}が希望の源だ
(Dreams [are] the source of hope.)
facts.explanation: こそ (this, especially (emphasis),very, emphasize the preceding word) — 夢こそが希望の源だ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Dreams — and not other things — are singled out as the true source of hope. こそ gives dreams that emphatic, exclusive quality.

facts.quizQuestion:
わたし{___}お詫びしなければなりません。
(It is [I], and not you, who must apologize.)
facts.explanation: こそ (this, especially (emphasis),very, emphasize the preceding word) — わたしこそお詫びしなければなりません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: It is specifically the speaker, not the listener, who should apologize — こそ focuses attention sharply on わたし.

facts.quizQuestion:
家族{___}が人生で最も大切なものだ
(It is [indeed] family that is the most important thing in life.)
facts.explanation: こそ (this, especially (emphasis),very, emphasize the preceding word) — 家族こそが人生で最も大切なものだ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Family is singled out as truly the most important thing in life — こそ gives this the feeling of a deep, sincere declaration.

facts.quizQuestion:
僕は決して農業が嫌いな{___}じゃない。
(By no means do I dislike farming. (Lit: The [reason] is not that I dislike farming)
facts.explanation: 訳 (meaning; reason; circumstances) — 僕は決して農業が嫌いな訳じゃない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Acts as a logical connector — the reason/circumstances are that I don't dislike farming, making 'by no means' make sense.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は{___}もなく悲しくなった。
(I felt sad for no [reason].)
facts.explanation: 訳 (meaning; reason; circumstances) — 私は訳もなく悲しくなった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Fills in the missing reason — the sentence says 'for no reason/circumstances,' so わけ is the word being negated here.

facts.quizQuestion:
契約{___}、雇用期間を三年とする。
([In accordance with] the contract, your tenure is three years.)
facts.explanation: に従って; に従い (following; in accordance with; as 〜, accordingly; in proportion to) — 契約に従い、雇用期間を三年とする。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.reading: にしたがって; にしたがい
facts.pronunciation: にしたがって; にしたがい
facts.grammarNote: The employment term is set in formal compliance with the contract — に従い is the formal written variant of に従って, fitting a legal/official context.

facts.quizQuestion:
収入が増える{___}、支出も増える。
([As] our income increases, our expenditure increases [accordingly].)
facts.explanation: に従って; に従い (following; in accordance with; as 〜, accordingly; in proportion to) — 収入が増えるに従って、支出も増える。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.reading: にしたがって; にしたがい
facts.pronunciation: にしたがって; にしたがい
facts.grammarNote: Spending rises in step with income — に従って describes a parallel change where one thing increases in accordance with another.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は、「これを　洗って　{___}ないか　い」と　いった。
(She said, "Please wash this [for me].")
facts.explanation: くれる (呉れる) (to give me, to do for me, I was given) — 彼女は、「これを　洗って　くれないか　い」と　いった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She's asking someone to do something for her benefit — the speaker (or subject) receives the favor of being asked, so くれる fits.

facts.quizQuestion:
先生は私に本を{___}ました。
(My teacher [gave me] a book.)
facts.explanation: くれる (呉れる) (to give me, to do for me, I was given) — 先生は私に本をくれました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The teacher gave the book toward the speaker — the action flows from someone else down to me, which is exactly what くれる expresses.

facts.quizQuestion:
友達は私に本を買って{___}ました。
(My friend bought (and) [gave me] a book.)
facts.explanation: くれる (呉れる) (to give me, to do for me, I was given) — 友達は私に本を買ってくれました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The friend did the buying as a favor directed at the speaker — the benefit comes inward to me, making くれる the right choice.

facts.quizQuestion:
今日、来て{___}てありがとうございます。
(Thankyou for coming today. (more formal)
facts.explanation: くれる (呉れる) (to give me, to do for me, I was given) — 今日、来てくれてありがとうございます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Thanking someone for coming implies they did it as a favor for you — くれる frames their action as something received, adding warmth to the thanks.

facts.quizQuestion:
知り合いである{___}は関係ない
(It doesn't matter [if] (or whether) we know each other (lit. "or not")
facts.explanation: かどうか (whether, if) — 知り合いであるかどうかは関係ない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: It doesn't matter either way — known or not. かどうか packages both possibilities (yes or no) into one unit for the point to apply to.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたはルーシーが日本語を話すことができる{___}知っていますか。
(Do you know [if] Lucy can speak Japanese?)
facts.explanation: かどうか (whether, if) — あなたはルーシーが日本語を話すことができるかどうか知っていますか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker wants to know if Lucy can speak Japanese or not. かどうか asks about both the yes and no outcomes inside an indirect question.

facts.quizQuestion:
あの人に彼女がいる{___}知ってる？
(Do you know [if] he has a girlfriend?)
facts.explanation: かどうか (whether, if) — あの人に彼女がいるかどうか知ってる？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The question is whether he has a girlfriend or not. かどうか bundles both possibilities into a single indirect question.

facts.quizQuestion:
きのう彼はあなたがお元気{___}尋ねていました。
(He asked after you your health yesterday.)
facts.explanation: かどうか (whether, if) — きのう彼はあなたがお元気かどうか尋ねていました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He was asking about your health — whether you were well or not. かどうか embeds that yes/no question into the reported inquiry.

facts.quizQuestion:
このくつを試しにはいて、合う{___}みてみなさい。
(Try these shoes on and see [if] they fit you.)
facts.explanation: かどうか (whether, if) — このくつを試しにはいて、合うかどうかみてみなさい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The test is whether the shoes fit or not. かどうか frames both outcomes as what needs to be checked.

facts.quizQuestion:
実家に帰った{___}昔の学校に行ってみた。
(At the time I went back to my hometown, I also took the opportunity to go see my old school.)
facts.explanation: ついでに (while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B) — 実家に帰ったついでに昔の学校に行ってみた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: While already visiting the hometown, the school visit was a secondary bonus trip — ついでに means you seized the opportunity while already doing something else.

facts.quizQuestion:
仕事へ行く{___}、郵便局で手紙を出した。
([While] on my way to work, I mailed a letter at the post office)
facts.explanation: ついでに (while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B) — 仕事へ行くついでに、郵便局で手紙を出した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Mailing the letter was a secondary task done during an existing trip to work — ついでに captures doing B efficiently while already on the way for A.

facts.quizQuestion:
出て行く{___}頼むのは悪いけど、ゴミを捨ててくれる？
(I hate to ask you to do this, but [since you are going out anyway], would you get rid of the garbage bag?)
facts.explanation: ついでに (while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B) — 出て行くついでに頼むのは悪いけど、ゴミを捨ててくれる？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Since you're going out anyway, taking the garbage is a convenient side task — ついでに means making use of an already-planned activity to add another.

facts.quizQuestion:
パリへ行く{___}ロンドンに寄る
([Since I am going to go to Paris, anyway], I will also drop by London)
facts.explanation: ついでに (while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B) — パリへ行くついでにロンドンに寄る
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: London is a side stop while already making the trip to Paris — ついでに frames dropping by as a natural bonus, not the main purpose.

facts.quizQuestion:
デパートへ行った{___}、そこで開かれていた展覧会を見た。
([While] out at the department store, I also took the opportunity to see the exhibition being held there.)
facts.explanation: ついでに (while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B) — デパートへ行ったついでに、そこで開かれていた展覧会を見た。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The exhibition was seen opportunistically during an existing trip to the department store — ついでに shows the second activity piggybacked on the first.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は自分の成功を、良い教育を受けたこと{___}考えた。
(He attributed his success to the good teaching he had received.)
facts.explanation: によると (according to) — 彼は自分の成功を、良い教育を受けたことによると考えた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He credited the source of his success — his education. によると frames that attribution, pointing to the basis for his conclusion.

facts.quizQuestion:
友達の話し{___}ればあの映画はとても面白いということです。
([According to] what my friend said, that movie is very interesting.)
facts.explanation: によると (according to) — 友達の話しによればあの映画はとても面白いということです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The information about the movie comes from a friend's account. によると signals that this is secondhand, sourced from that conversation.

facts.quizQuestion:
米国NBC News、ロイターなどの報道{___}、ブッシュ米大統領が、新しい宇宙探査計画の発表を準備しているとのことだ。
([According to] America's NBC News, Reuters and such news agencies US President Bush is in the process of preparing an anouncement of a new plan for space exploration)
facts.explanation: によると (according to) — 米国NBC News、ロイターなどの報道によると、ブッシュ米大統領が、新しい宇宙探査計画の発表を準備しているとのことだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Multiple news agencies are the named sources here. によると introduces the information as coming from those specific outlets.

facts.quizQuestion:
電灯の発明はエジソン{___}されている。
(The invention of the electric light is attributed [to/by] Edison.)
facts.explanation: によると (according to) — 電灯の発明はエジソンによるとされている。によるとされている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Credit for the invention is attributed to Edison — によると marks this as received knowledge, not the speaker's direct claim.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の概算{___}家の新築費用は３０００万円です。
(He estimates that the new house will cost roughly thirty million yen.)
facts.explanation: によると (according to) — 彼の概算によると家の新築費用は３０００万円です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The cost estimate comes from his calculation. によると sets up his figures as the source of the stated amount.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたがフランスに行きたいということをトムから{___}
([LIT] You want to go to France (that fact) i [heard] the thing from Tom, I heard)
facts.explanation: を聞いた (I heard) — あなたがフランスに行きたいということをトムから聞いた
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Reports something you were told by a specific person — here, Tom directly told you the news about France.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは酒嫌いだと{___}
(I [heard] you don't like alcohol)
facts.explanation: を聞いた (I heard) — あなたは酒嫌いだと聞いた
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A simple report of information received from someone — you heard this about the person not liking alcohol.

facts.quizQuestion:
トムが金持ちの女性と結婚したこと{___}。
(I've [heard] of Tom's marriage to a rich woman.)
facts.explanation: を聞いた (I heard) — トムが金持ちの女性と結婚したことを聞いた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: States that you received information about a specific event — Tom's marriage is something you learned about from an outside source.

facts.quizQuestion:
後ろから私の名前が呼ばれるの{___}。
(I [heard] my name called from behind.)
facts.explanation: を聞いた (I heard) — 後ろから私の名前が呼ばれるのを聞いた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Reports something you directly perceived with your senses — you literally heard your own name being called.

facts.quizQuestion:
昨日になって初めて、その事故のこと{___}。
(It was until yesterday that I [heard] about the accident.)
facts.explanation: を聞いた (I heard) — 昨日になって初めて、その事故のことを聞いた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: Marks when information was received — it wasn't until yesterday that this news about the accident actually reached you.

facts.quizQuestion:
これまで日本は外交{___}、閉鎖的な政策を取ってきた。
(Up until now, Japanese has taken a closed policy [toward] foreign countries.)
facts.explanation: に対して (toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per) — これまで日本は外交に対して、閉鎖的な政策を取ってきた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Japan's foreign policy is directed toward foreign affairs — に対して marks the target or addressee of a policy or stance.

facts.quizQuestion:
日本の大学に入学するのが難しいの{___}、アメリカの大学は卒業するのが難しい。
([Whereas] entering university is difficult in Japan, graduating from university is difficult in America.)
facts.explanation: に対して (toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per) — 日本の大学に入学するのが難しいのに対して、アメリカの大学は卒業するのが難しい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Entering university in Japan is contrasted with graduating in America — に対して sets up a direct comparison between two opposing situations.

facts.quizQuestion:
市民は増税{___}強く反対してる。
(The citizens are strongly opposed [to] the tax hike.)
facts.explanation: に対して (toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per) — 市民は増税に対して強く反対してる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Citizens direct their opposition toward the tax hike — に対して marks the target of their reaction or attitude.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は政治{___}強い関心がある。
(I have a strong interest [in] politics.)
facts.explanation: に対して (toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per) — 私は政治に対して強い関心がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker's interest is directed toward politics as its target — に対して identifies what the strong interest is aimed at.

facts.quizQuestion:
手数料は一万円{___}、五百円です。
(The commission charge will be 500 yen [per] 10,000 yen.)
facts.explanation: に対して (toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per) — 手数料は一万円に対して、五百円です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The 500-yen fee is calculated in proportion to each 10,000 yen — に対して expresses a rate or ratio relative to a given quantity.

facts.quizQuestion:
茶碗を口のところまで持っ{___}くるのは行儀が悪い。
([Bringing] the bowl up to your mouth is bad manners.)
facts.explanation: て (([verb]-te, connects two sentences)) — 茶碗を口のところまで持ってきて、箸でごはんをかきこむのは行儀が悪い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Two actions linked in sequence — first bringing the bowl up, then shoveling rice in. The て-form bridges these steps smoothly.

facts.quizQuestion:
この赤く{___}大きい車は田中さんのです。
(This large [and] red car is Mr Tanaka's.)
facts.explanation: て (([verb]-te, connects two sentences)) — この赤くて大きい車は田中さんのです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Two qualities of the car are listed together: red AND large. The て-form strings adjectives into one description.

facts.quizQuestion:
本屋に行っ{___}買います。
(I'm going to the book shop [and] buying it.)
facts.explanation: て (([verb]-te, connects two sentences)) — 本屋に行って買います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Going to the bookshop and buying it are two steps in one trip. The て-form connects them as a natural sequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
メロンを食べ{___}、コーヒーを飲みました。
(He ate some melon [and] drank some coffee.)
facts.explanation: て (([verb]-te, connects two sentences)) — メロンを食べて、コーヒーを飲みました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eating melon and drinking coffee are two things done one after the other. The て-form shows this simple sequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
歩い{___}来ます。
(I come on foot [(walking)].)
facts.explanation: て (([verb]-te, connects two sentences)) — 歩いて来ます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Walking is the method of coming — the て-form connects the means (walking) to the action (coming) as one continuous flow.

facts.quizQuestion:
外から{___}撮ってもいいよ と言われたので，外に出て撮影しました。
(I was told that [if] you're taking it from outside I don't mind so I went out and took the shot.)
facts.explanation: なら (if) — 外からなら撮ってもいいよ と言われたので，外に出て撮影しました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A permission was granted given the specific situation (shooting from outside), so the speaker acted on that conditional — this pattern fits a situation-based 'if'.

facts.quizQuestion:
乗る{___}飲むな。
([If] you drive, don't drink.)
facts.explanation: なら (if/given the situation) — 乗るなら飲むな。Marks a contextual condition: if the listener intends to drive, the speaker offers advice tied to that premise.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The warning is tied directly to the situation of driving — if you're going to do that, then don't drink. A clear situational condition.

facts.quizQuestion:
自分の信念を隠すくらい{___}死んだほうがましだ。
(I would rather die than conceal my belief.)
facts.explanation: なら (if) — 自分の信念を隠すくらいなら死んだほうがましだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Frames concealing your beliefs as the condition being considered — given that situation, death would be preferable.

facts.quizQuestion:
よんどころない理由で参加されないの{___}前払い金はお返しします。
([If] you cannot attend for unavoidable reasons, we will refund your advance payment.)
facts.explanation: なら (if) — よんどころない理由で参加されないのなら前払い金はお返しします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The refund policy kicks in only given a specific circumstance (unavoidable reasons) — the condition sets up the outcome.

facts.quizQuestion:
ご都合により参加されないの{___}前払い金はお返ししません。
([If] you cannot attend for reasons of your own, the advance payment will not be refunded.)
facts.explanation: なら (if) — ご都合により参加されないのなら前払い金はお返ししません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Again a refund condition, but this time based on personal reasons — the policy depends on the situation presented.

facts.quizQuestion:
そんなこと{___}なんて、あんたも強情だね。
(You must be pretty hardheaded to [make] him do that.)
facts.explanation: させる (make, let someone do something) — そんなことさせるなんて、あんたも強情だね。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Somebody made someone else do something, which counts as stubborn or pushy behavior. させる marks that causal force.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたの息子さんを親離れ{___}べきです。
(You should [make] your son independent from you.)
facts.explanation: させる (make, let someone do something) — あなたの息子さんを親離れさせるべきです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The son needs to be made independent — the parent should cause that to happen. させる frames the parent as the one driving the change.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたに心配{___}つもりがない。
(I don't mean to [make] you worry.)
facts.explanation: させる (make, let someone do something) — あなたに心配させるつもりがない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker doesn't want to cause you to worry — させる shows that the worry would be caused by the speaker's actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
どうしても私の言葉を彼に信じ{___}事ができなかった。
(I could never [make] him believe what I said.)
facts.explanation: させる (make, let someone do something) — どうしても私の言葉を彼に信じさせる事ができなかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Despite all efforts, the speaker couldn't get him to believe them — させる shows the failed attempt to cause belief in someone else.

facts.quizQuestion:
「ロミオ{___}ジュリエット」を見るたびに涙ぐむ。
(Every time I watch 'Romeo and Juliet' I tear up.)
facts.explanation: と ((on when) doing, if) — 「ロミオとジュリエット」を見ると必ず涙ぐむ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Watching the movie leads automatically and inevitably to tears — this pattern links a trigger to its natural, expected result.

facts.quizQuestion:
家を出る{___}すぐに雨が降り始めた。
([As soon as] I left home, it began to rain.)
facts.explanation: と ((on when) doing, if) — 家を出るとすぐに雨が降り始めた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: As soon as leaving the house, rain started — と marks an immediate, automatic sequence where one event naturally triggers the next.

facts.quizQuestion:
ここだ{___}２万円ほどでテレビが買えます。
([If] you come here you can buy a TV for around 20,000 yen.)
facts.explanation: と ((on when) doing, if) — ここだと２万円前後の テレビ が 買うことができる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Buying here automatically gives you the price range — と shows the natural consequence that follows from being at this location.

facts.quizQuestion:
冬になる{___}雨が降っています。
([When] it becomes winter (when winter comes) - it rains.)
facts.explanation: と ((on when) doing, if) — 冬になると雨が降っています。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Winter coming reliably brings rain — と expresses a natural, recurring conditional where the first event predictably leads to the second.

facts.quizQuestion:
立川駅に着く{___}電話をしなくてはいけない
([When] (I) get to Tachikawa station, I have to make a call.)
facts.explanation: と ((on when) doing, if) — 立川駅に着くと電話をしなくてはいけない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Arriving at the station automatically triggers the need to call — と shows the natural next step that follows as a direct consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
赤ちゃんに絵本を読んであ{___}
(I will read a picture book [for] the baby)
facts.explanation: 上げる (to do something for someone else) — 赤ちゃんに絵本を読んであげる
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: You're doing the reading for the baby's benefit, not your own — てあげる marks an action you perform as a gift or service for someone else.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたがたの近所の人たちをできるだけ助けてあ{___}ようにしましょう。
(You should do all you can to help [your neighbours].)
facts.explanation: 上げる (to do something for someone else) — あなたがたの近所の人たちをできるだけ助けてあげるようにしましょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Helping neighbors is framed as something you do for their sake — てあげる signals the action is an outward gift of effort toward others.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたにしてあ{___}事は、これだけです。
(This is all I can do [for you].)
facts.explanation: 上げる (to do something for someone else) — あなたにしてあげる事は、これだけです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This is all the speaker can do for the listener — てあげる shows the action is offered as a favor directed outward to that person.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたに代わって買い物をして家を掃除して夕食を作ってあ{___}。
(I'll do your shopping, clean up the house, and cook your dinner [for you].)
facts.explanation: 上げる (to do something for someone else) — あなたに代わって買い物をして家を掃除して夕食を作ってあげる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: All three tasks (shopping, cleaning, cooking) are done on your behalf — てあげる groups them together as favors the speaker is giving to you.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつかそのうちに動物園に連れてってあ{___}よ。
(I will take you to the zoo one of these days [for you].)
facts.explanation: 上げる (to do something for someone else) — いつかそのうちに動物園に連れてってあげるよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Promising to take someone to the zoo is a kind act done for them — てあげる expresses that this future action is a gift to the other person.

facts.quizQuestion:
君の{___}、お金を全部使ってしまい歩いて帰宅せねばならなかった。
([Thanks to] you, I spent all my money and had to walk home.)
facts.explanation: おかげで (thanks to) — 君のおかげで、お金を全部使ってしまい歩いて帰宅せねばならなかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used with biting irony — the speaker credits the other person for a chain of events that led to something bad happening to them.

facts.quizQuestion:
ひらがな{___}読めない。もちろん漢字も読めない。
(Cannot [even] read hiragana, and also of course cannot read kanji)
facts.explanation: からして ((1)Even; (2)Given, (Judging) From/By) — ひらがなからして読めない。もちろん漢字も読めない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Starts with the most basic skill as an example of total failure — if even hiragana is impossible, everything harder is too.

facts.quizQuestion:
A国は食料{___}、不足している。（暗示：だからガソリンなど、ほとんどないだろう）
(That country doesn't have enough food [for a start] (implies: so they definitely don't have enough gasoline etc.)
facts.explanation: からして ((1)Even; (2)Given, (Judging) From/By) — A国は食料からして、不足している。（暗示：だからガソリンなど、ほとんどないだろう）
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Takes the most essential resource as the starting point — if food is already short, it sets the baseline for everything else being scarce.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の健康状態{___}スポーツは無理でしょう。
([Judging from] his physical condition　doing sports would be unwise)
facts.explanation: からして ((1)Even; (2)Given, (Judging) From/By) — 彼の健康状態からしてスポーツは無理でしょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Uses someone's health as the observable basis for a conclusion — what you can see leads directly to the judgment being made.

facts.quizQuestion:
態度{___}けしからん
([Even] his attitude is nasty.)
facts.explanation: からして ((1)Even; (2)Given, (Judging) From/By) — 態度からしてけしからん
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Leads with the first bad thing to signal that everything about this person is problematic — even the attitude is bad.

facts.quizQuestion:
値段{___}、Xの機種はYとは比べものにならないと思っていた。
([Given] its price, I didn't expect the X machine to be in the same league as the Y machine.)
facts.explanation: からして ((1)Even; (2)Given, (Judging) From/By) — 値段からして、Xの機種はYとは比べものにならないと思っていた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Takes the price as the first indicator used to form an overall judgment — the cost alone shaped expectations about quality.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は風邪をひいた{___}です。
([Seems like] he's caught a cold.)
facts.explanation: みたい (looks like) — 彼は風邪をひいたみたいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used casually when you observe signs of something — you're not certain he caught a cold, but he sure seems like it.

facts.quizQuestion:
まるで夢{___}
(It's [like] a dream)
facts.explanation: みたい (looks like) — まるで夢みたい
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Compares the situation informally to something dreamlike — it's a casual, personal impression rather than a factual claim.

facts.quizQuestion:
お前{___}な奴は見たことがない
(I've never met the [likes] of you!)
facts.explanation: みたい (looks like) — お前みたいな奴は見たことがない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Creates a casual comparison — the speaker is saying they've never met anyone quite like this person before.

facts.quizQuestion:
京都{___}な所に行くのが好きです
(I like to visit places [like] Kyoto)
facts.explanation: みたい (looks like) — 京都みたいな所に行くのが好きです
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Draws a loose similarity — places that feel like Kyoto, with that same kind of atmosphere, are what the speaker enjoys.

facts.quizQuestion:
外国に行く{___}なことを言っていました
(He was talking [like] he was going abroad)
facts.explanation: みたい (looks like) — 外国に行くみたいなことを言っていました
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Reports the impression of what someone's words sounded like — his talk gave the casual feeling that he might go abroad.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は列車に乗り遅れた。もっと早く来る{___}だった。
(I missed the train. I [should] have come earlier.)
facts.explanation: べき (should do) — 私は列車に乗り遅れた。もっと早く来るべきだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He missed the train and now regrets not coming earlier. べき expresses the rational thing he should have done but didn't.

facts.quizQuestion:
われわれはなるべくたくさん本を読む{___}である。
(We [should] read as many books as possible.)
facts.explanation: べき (should do) — われわれはなるべくたくさん本を読むべきである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Reading widely is presented as the proper, sensible thing to do. べき carries a moral or educational 'ought to' that fits this advice.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは他の人たちの援助を当てにす{___}でない。
(You [shouldn't] rely on other people's help.)
facts.explanation: べき (should do) — あなたは他の人たちの援助を当てにすべきでない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Relying on others is framed as the wrong approach. べきでない is the natural way to say 'you shouldn't' when it's about rational judgment.

facts.quizQuestion:
いかに生きる{___}かは、青年にとって重要な問題である。
(How to live is an important question to young people.)
facts.explanation: べき (should do) — いかに生きるべきかは、青年にとって重要な問題である。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: How to live is the question — べき here frames it as 'the way one ought to live,' a philosophical should rather than a rule.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつ出発する{___}か私に教えてください。
(Please tell me when I [should] leave.)
facts.explanation: べき (should do) — いつ出発するべきか私に教えてください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker wants to know the right time to depart. べき asks for what would be the proper or best choice.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは、スミス氏と連絡を取り{___}べきです。
(You should keep in [touch] with Mr. Smith.)
facts.explanation: 合う (Vm, verb~together, mutually) — あなたは、スミス氏と連絡を取り合うべきです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Keeping in touch with Mr. Smith is a mutual, back-and-forth action — 合う adds the 'with each other' dimension to the verb 取り合う.

facts.quizQuestion:
お互いを理解し{___}ようにしようではありませんか。
(Let's try to understand [one another].)
facts.explanation: 合う (Vm, verb~together, mutually) — お互いを理解し合うようにしようではありませんか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Understanding one another requires both sides to act mutually — 合う turns 理解する into a reciprocal action between people.

facts.quizQuestion:
お母さんは私に彼と付き{___}なと言いました。
(My mother told me not to keep [company] with him.)
facts.explanation: 合う (Vm, verb~together, mutually) — お母さんは私に彼と付き合うなと言いました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: 付き合う means to keep company or associate with someone — 合う is the core component that makes this a mutual relationship verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
秘密でしたか？{___}うっかり喋ちゃいました。
(It was a secret? Oops! I spoke about it [without thinking]...)
facts.explanation: つい (without thinking, despite myself) — 秘密でしたか？ついうっかり喋ちゃいました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The slip of a secret fits perfectly — the speaker blurted it out without intending to, a classic unthinking moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}買っちゃった！
(Damn! I went and bought it [despite myself] (and I shouldn't have).)
facts.explanation: つい (without thinking, despite myself) — つい買っちゃった！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Captures a spontaneous regrettable purchase — the speaker did something impulsive, not carefully planned.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}「コンピュータがおかしい」と思ってしまいます．でも，コンピュータ側がおかしいことはまずありません．
(I [carelessly] thought "There's something up with the computer". But actually there was nothing up with the _computer_ side.)
facts.explanation: つい (without thinking, despite myself) — つい「コンピュータがおかしい」と思ってしまいます．でも，コンピュータ側がおかしいことはまずありません．
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Shows an automatic, unhelpful assumption — the speaker's first reaction was to blame the computer without really thinking.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}先ほど時雨れた
(We had a light shower a while ago.)
facts.explanation: つい (without thinking, despite myself) — つい先ほど時雨れた
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used here for 'just a moment ago' — the rain shower happened a short time back, almost without you noticing.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}必要なワードフィルを削除してしまった。
(I [just] (unintentional) deleted an important word file.)
facts.explanation: つい (without thinking, despite myself) — つい必要なワードフィルを削除してしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A classic careless action — the speaker deleted an important file without stopping to think about what they were doing.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が来る事を確かめる{___}に電話をした。
(I telephoned [to make sure] that he was coming.)
facts.explanation: 為 (In order to, because of, for) — 彼が来る事を確かめる為に電話をした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Calling first had a concrete purpose: confirming his arrival. ために links the phone call directly to that specific goal.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は、去年老齢の{___}、なくなった。
(He died last year [of] old age.)
facts.explanation: 為 (In order to, because of, for) — 彼は、去年老齢の為、なくなった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Old age is the direct cause of death here. ために in this context marks the reason or cause, not a purpose.

facts.quizQuestion:
このキーは何の{___}の物ですか？
(What's this key [for]?)
facts.explanation: 為 (In order to, because of, for) — このキーは何の為の物ですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The question asks what function the key serves. 何のために asks 'for what purpose' — pointing to its intended use.

facts.quizQuestion:
外国人の{___}のいい辞書がない
(There are no good dictionaries [for] foreigners)
facts.explanation: 為 (In order to, because of, for) — 外国人の為のいい辞書がない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: There's no dictionary designed to serve foreigners' needs. ための marks the intended beneficiary or purpose the dictionary would fulfill.

facts.quizQuestion:
開拓者達は苦労が多かった{___}、彼らの子供も同じだった。
(The pioneers had many hardships, [and] their children did too.)
facts.explanation: し (and) — 開拓者達は辛い生活をしたし、彼らの子供もそうだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Stacks one fact alongside another — pioneer life was hard, and adding to that, so was the children's life.

facts.quizQuestion:
この議論はおも{___}ろい上に、ときには困ったり、ぞっとさせられた。
(The discussion was interesting, [and] at times bewildering, even chilling.)
facts.explanation: し (and) — ここでの討論はおもしろいものでしたし、困惑させたり、ときにはぞっとする内容のものもありました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Lists qualities in sequence — the debate was interesting, and on top of that, confusing, and even horrifying.

facts.quizQuestion:
ロッククライミングもやった{___}、海で深く潜ったこともある。ジャングルで眠った夜もある。
(I've done rock climbing, [and] dived deep in the sea. I've also slept in the jungle.)
facts.explanation: し (and) — ロッククライミングも、海で深く潜ることもしたし、インドネシアの熱帯雨林で眠ったこともある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Connects completed experiences in a list — rock climbing was done, and deep diving was done, adding to the picture.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は幸せだ。給料が上がった{___}、家庭も持ったばかりだから。
(He is happy. His pay went up, [and] he just started a family.)
facts.explanation: し (and) — 彼は幸福だ。1つには、昇進したし、それに結婚したばかりだから。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Adds a second contributing reason to the first — the promotion is one reason he's happy, and the marriage is another.

facts.quizQuestion:
母はそのニュースを喜んでいた{___}、私も同じだった。
(My mother was happy about the news, [and] so was I.)
facts.explanation: し (and) — 母はその知らせを喜んでいたし、私もそうでした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Connects two people sharing the same reaction — the mother was happy, and the speaker adds that they felt the same way.

facts.quizQuestion:
スポーツの{___}どちらが一番好き？
([out of all] the sports, which do you like best?)
facts.explanation: 中に、〜上に、〜下に、など (Talking about relative location of an object) — スポーツの中に、どちらが一番好き？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Asks which one among a set — 'out of all the sports' uses 中に to frame the group you're selecting from.

facts.quizQuestion:
２つの問題が未解決の{___}であった。
(Two problems remained [unsolved].)
facts.explanation: まま (as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged) — ２つの問題が未解決のままであった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The problems stayed in their unsolved condition without changing — まま describes a state that persists unchanged from how it was.

facts.quizQuestion:
この家はこの{___}にしておきたい。
(I like this house [as it is].)
facts.explanation: まま (as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged) — この家はこのままにしておきたい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Keeping the house exactly as it currently is — まま expresses wanting no change to an existing state.

facts.quizQuestion:
この家は今の{___}では売れないだろう。
(This house will not sell [as it stands].)
facts.explanation: まま (as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged) — この家は今のままでは売れないだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The house can't sell in its current unchanged condition — まま describes the state as-is, implying it has not been improved or altered.

facts.quizQuestion:
この自転車は今月の初めからここに置かれた{___}だ。
(This bicycle has [been left] here since the beginning of this month.)
facts.explanation: まま (as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged) — この自転車は今月の初めからここに置かれたままだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The bicycle has been sitting there since early in the month, untouched — まま shows it was placed there and left in that same state ever since.

facts.quizQuestion:
この窓は一ヶ月割れた{___}になっている。
(This window has [been broken] for a month.)
facts.explanation: まま (as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged) — この窓は一ヶ月割れたままになっている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The window has been sitting broken for a month without being fixed — まま describes the unchanged broken state continuing over that time.

facts.quizQuestion:
あっとい{___}に砂嵐に襲われた。
([Before we knew] where we were, the dust storm was on us.)
facts.explanation: あっと言う間 (In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies) — あっという間に砂嵐に襲われた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The dust storm arrived so fast there was no time to react — it was on them before they even processed it was coming.

facts.quizQuestion:
あっとい{___}に逃げてしまった。
(He disappeared [in an instant].)
facts.explanation: あっと言う間 (In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies) — あっという間に逃げてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The escape happened so quickly it barely registered — one moment he was there, the next he was gone.

facts.quizQuestion:
クリスマスの時期にはあっとい{___}に満席になるから早く飛行機の蘭??ｵなさい。
(Book your flight early as it fills up [quickly] during Christmas.)
facts.explanation: あっと言う間 (In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies) — クリスマスの時期にはあっという間に満席になるから早く飛行機の蘭??ｵなさい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Flights fill up shockingly fast during Christmas — you need to book early or you'll miss your chance in an instant.

facts.quizQuestion:
クレジットカードを使うとあっとい{___}に借金をつくりやすい。
(It's easy to get into debt [quickly] with charge cards.)
facts.explanation: あっと言う間 (In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies) — クレジットカードを使うとあっという間に借金をつくりやすい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: Debt can pile up before you realize it — credit cards make it dangerously easy to spend too much in a flash.

facts.quizQuestion:
鳥は{___}に飛び去った。
(The bird flew away [in a flash].)
facts.explanation: あっと言う間 (In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies) — 鳥はあっと言う間に飛び去った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Birds take off without warning — the moment the bird went, it was just gone, instantly.

facts.quizQuestion:
日本へ行く{___}れば
(If we [assume] that we go to japan)
facts.explanation: とする (assume, regard X as ~ , suppose) — 日本へ行くとすれば
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Sets up a hypothetical scenario for reasoning — pretending we go to Japan in order to think through what follows.

facts.quizQuestion:
温度は一定である{___}。
(We [assume] that temperature is constant.)
facts.explanation: とする (assume, regard X as ~ , suppose) — 温度は一定であるとする。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Treats a condition as a fixed assumption for a calculation or model — temperature is held constant for the purposes of the argument.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたが立派な世界市民になろう{___}なら、偏見は持たないように努力すべきである。
(If you are [to be] a good world citizen, you should try to be free of any prejudices.)
facts.explanation: とする (assume, regard X as ~ , suppose) — あなたが立派な世界市民になろうとするなら、偏見は持たないように努力すべきである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes being on the path toward a goal — trying to become a good world citizen is the ongoing aim driving the advice.

facts.quizQuestion:
私たちは みな、 人生に おいて 成功を 収めよう{___}が、成功するのは、きわめて 数少ない人たちである。
(We all [try hard] to make the grade in life, but only a few succeed.)
facts.explanation: とする (assume, regard X as ~ , suppose) — 私たちは みな、 人生に おいて 成功を 収めようとするが、成功するのは、きわめて 数少ない人たちである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Captures the universal effort toward success — everyone is actively attempting to achieve it, even if few make it.

facts.quizQuestion:
進んで学ぼう{___}気の無い物には教える甲斐がない。
(One who is not [willing] to learn is not worth teaching.)
facts.explanation: とする (assume, regard X as ~ , suppose) — 進んで学ぼうとする気の無い物には教える甲斐がない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Highlights the absence of genuine effort — someone who isn't really trying to learn isn't worth the trouble of teaching.

facts.quizQuestion:
私が書いた{___}を問わず、(全て）「作者：amatsuka」と表記します。
(Regardless of [whether] I wrote it or not, the byline is always "Author: amatsuka".)
facts.explanation: か (whether, the question of (sub sentence question)) — 私が書いたかを問わず、(全て）「作者：あまつか」です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Whether or not they wrote it, the author credit stays the same. か embeds the question of authorship as a subordinate clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
おまえは・・・俺が誰{___}知っているらしい。
(You ... seem to know [who] I am.)
facts.explanation: か (whether, the question of (sub sentence question)) — おまえは・・・・俺が誰か知ってるのか。
