facts.grammarNote: Daily Japanese study is framed as something the speaker must do. なければなりません expresses this as a firm, polite obligation.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは日本の音楽を聞く{___}上手になります。
([By listening to] Japanese music, you will become better.)
facts.explanation: ことで (by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means)) — あなたは日本の音楽を聞くことで上手になります。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Shows that the action of listening is the method that produces the improvement — the activity itself is what drives the result.

facts.quizQuestion:
退席する{___}実は正しいことをすることになる。
(You may actually be doing the right thing [by walking out].)
facts.explanation: ことで (by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means)) — 退席することで実は正しいことをすることになる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Frames walking out as the means by which something right gets accomplished — the action is the mechanism behind the outcome.

facts.quizQuestion:
まいにちれんしゅうする{___}、にほんごがぺらぺらになりました。
(Everyday [by studying] Japanese language, you will be better.)
facts.explanation: ことで (by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means)) — まいにちれんしゅうすることで、にほんごがぺらぺらになりました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Identifies daily practice as the direct method behind becoming fluent — the activity is what makes the outcome possible.

facts.quizQuestion:
悪魔め！俺様は聖{___}剣で貴様を倒す！
(Foul demon! I will smite you with this [sacred] sword!)
facts.explanation: なる (Used much like the な of na-adjectives - somewhat archaic.) — 悪魔め！俺様は聖なる剣で貴様を倒す！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: 聖なる is a poetic/archaic way to say 'sacred' — なる is used here instead of な for a dramatic, literary feel.

facts.quizQuestion:
スター・ウォーズのエピソード４の副題は、「新た{___}希望」である。
(The subtitle of Episode IV of Star Wars is "a [new] hope".)
facts.explanation: なる (Used much like the な of na-adjectives - somewhat archaic.) — スター・ウォーズのエピソード４の副題は、「新たなる希望」である。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The subtitle 'A New Hope' uses 新たなる — an older, more ceremonial way to say 'new,' fitting the epic tone of the film.

facts.quizQuestion:
うちは毎年９月末の日曜日{___}と決まっているのです。
(It's our rule to [make it] the last Sunday of September every year.)
facts.explanation: にする (To decide on, to choose) — うちは毎年９月末の日曜日にすると決まっているのです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The family has a fixed rule about which day to use, and にする expresses choosing and settling on that specific option.

facts.quizQuestion:
そのよう{___}と後で困らない？
(if you [do it] this way, won't you be troubled later on?)
facts.explanation: にする (To decide on, to choose) — そのようにすると後で困らない？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Doing it 'this way' is the choice being made — にする marks the decision to go with a particular option or approach.

facts.quizQuestion:
私 は 食べ{___}
(I [am eating] now.)
facts.explanation: て形+いる (て居る) (Vte (Continuing states, experience, repetative events)/ doing currently) — 私 は 食べている
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eating is happening right now at the moment of speaking — ている attaches to an action in progress to show it is currently ongoing.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなた は 見{___}
(You [are looking] now.)
facts.explanation: て形+いる (て居る) (Vte (Continuing states, experience, repetative events)/ doing currently) — あなた は 見ている
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Looking is happening at this moment — ている marks the action as currently in progress, the simplest ongoing-action usage of this form.

facts.quizQuestion:
とても寒いけれど、彼女はコート{___}で出かけました。
(Although it is very cold, she went out [without] a coat on.)
facts.explanation: なし (without) — とても寒いけれど、彼女はコートなしで出かけました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She went out in a coatless state — なし attaches to a noun to mean 'without that thing,' describing the absence of the coat.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女はコート{___}で出かけました
(she went out [without] wearing a coat.)
facts.explanation: なし (without) — 彼女はコートなしで出かけました
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She left wearing no coat — なし marks the coat as something absent, characterizing the state she was in when she went out.

facts.quizQuestion:
朝ごはんを食べ{___}学校へ来ました。
(I came to school [without] eating breakfast.)
facts.explanation: ずに (without) — 朝ごはんを食べずに学校へ来ました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Coming to school without eating breakfast means the eating was skipped — ずに attaches to a verb in negative form to mean 'without doing' that action.

facts.quizQuestion:
勉強せ{___}テストを受けた。
(I took the test [without] having studied.)
facts.explanation: ずに (without) — 勉強せずにテストを受けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Taking the test without having studied means studying was skipped — ずに marks studying as the action that was bypassed before the test.

facts.quizQuestion:
最近ちょっと太ったから毎日ハンバーガーを食べない{___}しました。
(I gained a little weight recently so I've [decided] not to eat hamburgers every day.)
facts.explanation: ことにする (Decide to do something) — 最近ちょっと太ったから毎日ハンバーガーを食べないことにしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The weight gain prompted a decision to change eating habits. ことにする is used when you make a choice for yourself about what you will do.

facts.quizQuestion:
合格{___}ようにもっと勉強しようと決めました。
(In order to pass the exam I decided to study harder.)
facts.explanation: ことにする (Decide to do something) — 合格するようにもっと勉強をすることにしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: 合格する is the target state the speaker wants to reach. ように sets up the goal in order to justify studying harder.

facts.quizQuestion:
たくさんあって､{___}食べきれません
(There is so much (food) that I [can't possibly] eat it all.)
facts.explanation: とても〜ない (Can`t possibly) — たくさんあって､とても食べきれません
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The amount of food makes finishing it simply impossible — とても sets up the impossibility reinforced by the negative verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
東京では{___}家なんか買えません。
(I [can't possibly] buy a house in Tokyo.)
facts.explanation: とても〜ない (Can`t possibly) — 東京ではとても家なんか買えません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Tokyo's prices are so high that buying a house there isn't realistic — the impossibility is total, not just difficult.

facts.quizQuestion:
主人が仕事で東京にいく{___}った。
([It has been decided that] my husband will go to Tokyo for professional reasons.)
facts.explanation: ことになる (it has been decided/arranged that) — 主人が仕事で東京にいくことになった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The husband going to Tokyo wasn't his own free choice — it was arranged or decided by circumstances, which is what ことになる conveys.

facts.quizQuestion:
留学する{___}りました。
([It has been arranged that] I will study abroad.)
facts.explanation: ことになる (it has been decided/arranged that) — 留学することになりました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Studying abroad was arranged or settled — the speaker presents it as something that came to be decided, not as a personal vow.

facts.quizQuestion:
昨夜、誰がテレビをつけた{___}
(I [wonder] who turned on the TV last night.)
facts.explanation: かしら。 (I wonder if...　（A type of question mark)) — 昨夜、誰がテレビをつけたかしら。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is wondering who left the TV on — a soft, self-directed mystery. かしら gives it a gentle, feminine wondering tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
いくら{___}
(I [wonder] how much it is.)
facts.explanation: かしら。 (I wonder if...　（A type of question mark)) — いくらかしら。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is wondering about the price to themselves. かしら gives this a soft, feminine 'I wonder' quality rather than a direct question.

facts.quizQuestion:
多くのアメリカ人{___}に彼は気さくだ。
([Like] many Americans, he is very friendly.)
facts.explanation: と同様 (the same way as, as well as) — 多くのアメリカ人と同様に彼は気さくだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Likens one thing directly to another — he behaves the same way as many Americans, so the comparison calls for this pattern.

facts.quizQuestion:
少しずつそうなった{___}
(its a gradual thing, [isn't it]?)
facts.explanation: ということですね (と云う事ですね) (your message is that ...) — 少しずつそうなったということですね
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.targetLanguageWord: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.reading: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.pronunciation: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is checking their understanding of what was said — ということですね confirms 'so your point is that this happened gradually?'

facts.quizQuestion:
この写真{___}カットが欲しい。
(I want a haircut [like] this one in the picture.)
facts.explanation: のような (〜の様な) (just like, alike to, similar to) — この写真のようなカットが欲しい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Points to a specific picture as the reference — you want a haircut that looks like the style in the photo.

facts.quizQuestion:
そろそろ終わり{___}？
(Is it about to end [?])
facts.explanation: かい。 ((A type of question mark)) — そろそろ終わりかい？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker asks a simple yes/no question about whether it's wrapping up soon. かい makes it sound casual and friendly, typical of relaxed male speech.

facts.quizQuestion:
電車の中での勉強はとても無理なような{___}します。
(I [feel like] it's almost impossible to study on the train.)
facts.explanation: 気がする (to feel, realize) — 電車の中での勉強はとても無理なような気がします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Expresses a gut feeling about studying on trains — not a fact, but a personal sense that it would be very difficult.

facts.quizQuestion:
旅行で行きましたけど、そこには住みたくないような{___}しました。
(I went travelling there, but [realized] I would not like to live there.)
facts.explanation: 気がする (to feel, realize) — 旅行で行きましたけど、そこには住みたくないような気がしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Reports an internal realization from experience — after visiting, the speaker came to feel they wouldn't want to live there.

facts.quizQuestion:
悪い人のような{___}しました。
(I [felt that] he was a bad person.)
facts.explanation: 気がする (to feel, realize) — 悪い人のような気がしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes an intuitive impression of a person — something about them gave the speaker a bad feeling, without proof.

facts.quizQuestion:
高いような{___}している。
(I [feel like] its expensive.)
facts.explanation: 気がする (to feel, realize) — 高いような気がしている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A subjective sense about price — the speaker can't confirm the cost, but it feels expensive to them.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつも排尿したいような{___}。
(I have a constant [feeling] I have to pee.)
facts.explanation: 気がする (to feel, realize) — いつも排尿したいような気がする。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: Describes a persistent bodily feeling — the sensation of needing to urinate keeps coming, not just once.

facts.quizQuestion:
そんな{___}はない
(That [cannot be supposed to be so].)
facts.explanation: 筈 (believe that it is so; expect that it is so; it is supposed to be so) — そんな筈はない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is asserting that something cannot possibly be true. そんな筈はない is a set phrase rejecting an expected outcome as impossible.

facts.quizQuestion:
年寄り{___}若く見える。
(Even though he's old, he looks young.)
facts.explanation: にしては (even though (it is) ; for) — 年寄りにしては若く見える。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: He looks young considering his age as an old person — にしては sets up an expectation based on the category (being old) that the observation defies.

facts.quizQuestion:
お金持ち{___}古い車に乗っている。
([For] (eg: even though he is) a rich person, he drives an old car.)
facts.explanation: にしては (even though (it is) ; for) — お金持ちにしては古い車に乗っている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Driving an old car is unexpected for someone rich — にしては judges the behavior against what you'd expect given the person's status.

facts.quizQuestion:
６月{___}寒い朝だ。
(It is a cold morning [for] June.)
facts.explanation: にしては (even though (it is) ; for) — ６月にしては寒い朝だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Cold mornings are unexpected for June — にしては frames June as the standard we measure against and calls the coldness surprising.

facts.quizQuestion:
７０歳{___}、彼はいまだに驚くほど元気である。
([For] a man of seventy he still has surprising vigour.)
facts.explanation: にしては (even though (it is) ; for) — ７０歳にしては、彼はいまだに驚くほど元気である。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: Being vigorous at 70 defies the expected limitations of that age — にしては uses the age as the benchmark against which the vigor is surprising.

facts.quizQuestion:
このホテルはこの町{___}比較的高い。
(This hotel is relatively expensive [for] this town.)
facts.explanation: にしては (even though (it is) ; for) — このホテルはこの町にしては比較的高い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The hotel is pricey relative to what you'd expect for this town — にしては measures the price against the expected norm for the town.

facts.quizQuestion:
高い物が必ずしも安いものより良い{___}。
(Expensive things are [not always] better than cheap things.)
facts.explanation: とは限らない (not always, not necessarily) — 高い物が必ずしも安いものより良いとは限らない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Rejects a blanket assumption — just because something is expensive doesn't automatically mean it's better. The sentence is making that point.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつでも自分の思い通りにできる{___}よ。
(You cannot [always] have your own way.)
facts.explanation: とは限らない (not always, not necessarily) — いつでも自分の思い通りにできるとは限らないよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Pushes back on the idea that you can always get your own way — it's not guaranteed, and this pattern captures that soft denial.

facts.quizQuestion:
うまい冗談を言うことはいつもたやすい{___}。
(It is [not always] easy to make a good joke.)
facts.explanation: とは限らない (not always, not necessarily) — うまい冗談を言うことはいつもたやすいとは限らない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Points out that making a good joke isn't reliably easy — it's not always the case, which is exactly what this pattern expresses.

facts.quizQuestion:
おいしそうに見える食べ物が必ずしもおいしい{___}。
(Delicious looking food doesn't [necessarily] taste good.)
facts.explanation: とは限らない (not always, not necessarily) — おいしそうに見える食べ物が必ずしもおいしいとは限らない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The sentence challenges the idea that looking tasty equals tasting good — something isn't necessarily what it seems.

facts.quizQuestion:
お金が幸福をもたらす{___}。
(Money does [not always] bring happiness.)
facts.explanation: とは限らない (not always, not necessarily) — お金が幸福をもたらすとは限らない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Money bringing happiness is treated as a general rule that doesn't always hold — this pattern flags that the assumption isn't absolute.

facts.quizQuestion:
英語が{___}わかりません。
(I don't understand English [very well].)
facts.explanation: あまり　+　動詞(否定形) (not very, not much) — 英語があまりわかりません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.targetLanguageWord: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Softens a weak skill — the speaker isn't saying they understand nothing, just that their English isn't very good.

facts.quizQuestion:
今回のことで{___}思い詰めないほうがいいよ。だれにでも失敗はあるんだから。
(Don't worry [too much] about it. Everybody blows it once in a while.)
facts.explanation: あまり　+　動詞(否定形) (not very, not much) — 今回のことであまり思い詰めないほうがいいよ。だれにでも失敗はあるんだから。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Advises moderation — the speaker is telling someone not to overthink a mistake, to a limited degree only.

facts.quizQuestion:
ホテルのメイドとしてパートで仕事していますが、その仕事は{___}好きではありません。
(I had a part-time job as a hotel maid, but I didn't like it [very much])
facts.explanation: あまり　+　動詞(否定形) (not very, not much) — ホテルのメイドとしてパートで仕事していますが、その仕事はあまり好きではありません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Expresses mild dislike — the speaker didn't hate the job, but they weren't fond of it either.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは{___}にも素早すぎる。
(You are all [too] quick.)
facts.explanation: あまり　+　動詞(否定形) (not very, not much) — あなたはあまりにも素早すぎる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Qualifies excessive speed — 'all too quick' means the quickness is at a degree that goes a bit too far.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは{___}夜更かしをしない方がいい。
(You had better not sit up [late] up night.)
facts.explanation: あまり　+　動詞(否定形) (not very, not much) — あなたはあまり夜更かしをしない方がいい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Recommends restraint — staying up isn't totally forbidden, just advised against doing it too much.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}に心配しないでください。
(Please don't worry [like this](so hard).)
facts.explanation: こんな (such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind) — こんなに心配しないでください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Points to the current level of worry and says stop — the worrying happening right now, this much, should end.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}面白いことはない
(Nothing is more interesting (than [this])
facts.explanation: こんな (such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind) — こんな面白いことはない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Uses the current thing as the ultimate benchmark — nothing tops this in terms of interest.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}に夜遅く
(so late at night (as [this]).)
facts.explanation: こんな (such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind) — こんなに夜遅く
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: References the actual hour as the extreme — it's late at night right now, as late as this very moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}風に持って下さい
(Please hold it in [this] way)
facts.explanation: こんな (such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind) — こんな風に持って下さい
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Points to the exact way something is being held right now — hold it in this specific manner I'm showing you.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}ことになろうとは、正に「お釈迦様でも気がつくめい」だね。
(It's precisely "Even the Buddha could not have guessed it" in the sense that things turned out [this way].)
facts.explanation: こんな (such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind) — こんなことになろうとは、正に「お釈迦様でも気がつくめい」だね。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: References the unexpected situation that actually came about — even the Buddha couldn't have foreseen things turning out this particular way.

facts.quizQuestion:
この大学で教えられることは私{___}極めて名誉なことです。
(Being able to teach at this university is a big honor [for me].)
facts.explanation: にとって (to; for) — この大学で教えられることは私にとって極めて名誉なことです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Teaching at this university is an honor from the speaker's personal perspective — にとって marks the viewpoint from which something is evaluated.

facts.quizQuestion:
町の人々{___}この工場の閉鎖は死活問題だ。
([For people in this town], the shutdown of this plant is a matter of life and death.)
facts.explanation: にとって (to; for) — 町の人々にとってこの工場の閉鎖は死活問題だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The factory closure is a life-or-death matter from the townspeople's perspective — にとって marks whose point of view makes this a critical issue.

facts.quizQuestion:
ここは君{___}一番安全な場所だ。
(This is the safest place [for you].)
facts.explanation: にとって (to; for) — ここは君にとって一番安全な場所だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This is the safest place judged from your particular situation — にとって marks you as the reference point for what counts as safe.

facts.quizQuestion:
アジア人{___}乳製品は食べにくい
([for Asians], dairy products are hard to eat)
facts.explanation: にとって (to; for) — アジア人にとって乳製品は食べにくい
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Dairy being hard to eat is a judgment from the perspective of Asians — にとって marks the group whose experience makes this true.

facts.quizQuestion:
この写真は私{___}、何よりも大切なものです。
([For me], that photo is more important than anything.)
facts.explanation: にとって (to; for) — この写真は私にとって、何よりも大切なものです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The photo being most important is an assessment from the speaker's personal standpoint — にとって marks whose values and feelings define the importance.

facts.quizQuestion:
寝不足の{___}、今日は頭がぼんやりしている
([Due to] lack of sleep, today I'm absent minded.)
facts.explanation: せいで (because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)) — 寝不足のせいで、今日は頭がぼんやりしている
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Blames the lack of sleep as the negative cause — the foggy head is the unwanted result of something that went wrong.

facts.quizQuestion:
あいつの{___}、先生に叱られた。
([Because of] him, I was scolded by my teacher.)
facts.explanation: せいで (because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)) — あいつのせいで、先生に叱られた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Assigns responsibility for a bad outcome to another person — being scolded happened because of what he did.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は失敗したのは、彼の{___}だ。
([Because of] him, I failed.)
facts.explanation: せいで (because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)) — 私は失敗したのは、彼のせいだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Points the finger at another person as the reason for failure — his actions are blamed as the cause.

facts.quizQuestion:
電車が遅れた{___}、遅刻した。
(I was late [because] the train was late.)
facts.explanation: せいで (because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)) — 電車が遅れたせいで、遅刻した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Pins the lateness on the train delay — the bad outcome (being late) is attributed to something external that went wrong.

facts.quizQuestion:
暑い{___}か、食欲がない。
([Since] it's hot, I have no appetite.)
facts.explanation: せいで (because of, due to (indicates only negative meaning)) — 暑いせいか、食欲がない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Blames the heat as the reason for having no appetite — the unpleasant condition is what caused the loss of interest in food.

facts.quizQuestion:
どうぶつをいじめ{___}。
(One [must not] harass the animals.)
facts.explanation: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い) (you shouldn't, one should not) — どうぶつをいじめてはいけません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い)
facts.grammarNote: Harassing animals is prohibited. てはいけません is the standard polite form for expressing something is not allowed.

facts.quizQuestion:
ここでたばこを吸うっ{___}。禁煙ですから。
(You are [not allowed to] smoke here, because this is a no-smoke area.)
facts.explanation: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い) (you shouldn't, one should not) — ここでたばこを吸うってはいけません。禁煙ですから。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い)
facts.grammarNote: Smoking here is not permitted — the no-smoking rule applies. てはいけません states this prohibition politely.

facts.quizQuestion:
ここに自転車を止め{___}。
(It is [forbidden to] park bicycles here.)
facts.explanation: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い) (you shouldn't, one should not) — ここに自転車を止めてはいけない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い)
facts.grammarNote: Parking bicycles here is forbidden. てはいけない states this rule as a clear prohibition.

facts.quizQuestion:
「あなたは塩辛いものを食べ{___}よ」と言ったけど、彼は食べ物にたくさんの塩を加えた。
(Although I told him "You're [not allowed to] eat salty!" he added a lot of salt in his food.)
facts.explanation: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い) (you shouldn't, one should not) — 「あなたは塩辛いものを食べてはいけないよ」と言ったけど、彼は食べ物にたくさんの塩を加えた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+はいけない (ては行け無い)
facts.grammarNote: The person was told not to eat salty food — a prohibition issued for health reasons. てはいけない delivers that rule clearly.

facts.quizQuestion:
前から欲しかった本がやっと手に入ったものだから、早速徹夜して終わりまで読ん{___}。
(Because I finally came into possession of a book I was looking after for a long time, at once in a sleepless night I [finished] reading it until the end.)
facts.explanation: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う) (wind up ____ing, end up ___ing, finish ____ing) — 前から欲しかった本がやっと手に入ったものだから、早速徹夜して終わりまで読んでしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う)
facts.grammarNote: Staying up all night to finish the book shows total completion with a sense of 'I went and did it' — てしまう marks the action as fully concluded.

facts.quizQuestion:
その映画を見て泣い{___}。
(Watching that movie, I [ended up] crying.)
facts.explanation: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う) (wind up ____ing, end up ___ing, finish ____ing) — その映画を見て泣いてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う)
facts.grammarNote: Crying at the movie was unplanned and complete — てしまった captures the 'ended up doing it' feeling of an emotional result you didn't fully intend.

facts.quizQuestion:
宿題はもうやっ{___}。
(I've [finished] my homework.)
facts.explanation: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う) (wind up ____ing, end up ___ing, finish ____ing) — 宿題はもうやってしまいました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う)
facts.grammarNote: The homework is completely finished and done — てしまいました emphasizes full completion, often with a slightly proud or relieved tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつも本を読んでいるうちに眠っ{___}。
(I always [fall asleep] while reading a book.)
facts.explanation: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う) (wind up ____ing, end up ___ing, finish ____ing) — いつも本を読んでいるうちに眠ってしまう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う)
facts.grammarNote: Falling asleep every time while reading is an unintended habitual outcome — てしまう fits this recurring, slightly regrettable result.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は稼ぐ金をすべて本に使っ{___}。
(What money he earns he [ends up spending] on books.)
facts.explanation: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う) (wind up ____ing, end up ___ing, finish ____ing) — 彼は稼ぐ金をすべて本に使ってしまう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: て形+しまう (て仕舞う・て終う・て了う)
facts.grammarNote: He inevitably spends everything he earns on books — てしまう captures the sense that this complete, habitual spending happens as if he can't help it.

facts.quizQuestion:
この小説を子供{___}に書き改めるのは難しい。
(It is hard to adapt this story [for] children.)
facts.explanation: 向き (for, facing, toward, aimed at) — この小説を子供向きに書き改めるのは難しい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The story is being adapted to suit children as its target audience — 向き means aimed at or appropriate for a specific group.

facts.quizQuestion:
こういう本は若い読者{___}ではない。
(These books are not fit [for young readers].)
facts.explanation: 向き (for, facing, toward, aimed at) — こういう本は若い読者向きではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: These books are not aimed at or suitable for young readers — 向き judges fit or appropriateness for a target audience.

facts.quizQuestion:
このとおりやさしい英語で書かれているので、その本は初心者{___}である。
(Written as if is in plain English, the book is suitable [for beginners].)
facts.explanation: 向き (for, facing, toward, aimed at) — このとおりやさしい英語で書かれているので、その本は初心者向きである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The plain English makes the book suitable and aimed toward beginners — 向き signals the intended or appropriate audience for something.

facts.quizQuestion:
このテキストは初心者{___}にできている。
(This text is calculated [for beginners].)
facts.explanation: 向き (for, facing, toward, aimed at) — このテキストは初心者向きにできている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The text is designed with beginners as the intended audience — 向き describes who the material is built for or aimed at.

facts.quizQuestion:
この派手な服は私には{___}ません。
(This showy dress isn't appropriate [for me].)
facts.explanation: 向き (for, facing, toward, aimed at) — この派手な服は私には向きません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The flashy dress doesn't suit or fit the speaker's style — 向き here means appropriate for or fitting for a particular person.

facts.quizQuestion:
若い{___}本をたくさん読みなさい。
([While] you are young, read many books.)
facts.explanation: うちに (while; during; as) — 若いうちに本をたくさん読みなさい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Youth is a condition that won't last — うちに urges action while the current state (being young) still holds, before it changes.

facts.quizQuestion:
暇な{___}、遊びに来てください。
([While] you are free, come here and play with me please.)
facts.explanation: うちに (while; during; as) — 暇なうちに、遊びに来てください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Come visit while you're still free — うちに means to act during a window of time before the condition (free time) ends.

facts.quizQuestion:
暗黙の{___}承諾する。
(Accept implicitly [while you can].)
facts.explanation: うちに (while; during; as) — 暗黙のうちに承諾する。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Accepting something 'within' the bounds of tacit understanding — うちに here expresses the implicit frame or context within which agreement happens.

facts.quizQuestion:
みるみる{___}太った。
(He gained weight just [while] I was watching him.)
facts.explanation: うちに (while; during; as) — みるみるうちに太った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The weight gain happened so fast it was noticeable while watching — うちに captures the sense that it occurred within that brief window of observation.

facts.quizQuestion:
どうぞ、熱い{___}、食べて下さい。
(Please go ahead and eat it [while] it's still hot)
facts.explanation: うちに (while; during; as) — どうぞ、熱いうちに、食べて下さい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eat it while it's still hot — うちに urges action before the current state (hotness) changes, emphasizing the limited window.

facts.quizQuestion:
こっそりたばこを吸っている{___}妹に見られた。
(I was having a cheeky cigarette and [on that occasion], I was seen by my little sister)
facts.explanation: ところに、ところへ、ところを ((Just) when) — こっそりたばこを吸っているところを妹に見られた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.reading: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.pronunciation: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The sister saw him at the exact moment he was sneaking a cigarette — ところを marks an action caught in progress, often with a surveillance or discovery nuance.

facts.quizQuestion:
ぐっすり寝ている{___}地震が起きて、はっと目が覚めた。
(An earthquake hit [right when] I was soundly asleep, and I jerked awake.)
facts.explanation: ところに、ところへ、ところを ((Just) when) — ぐっすり寝ているところに地震が起きて、はっと目が覚めた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.reading: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.pronunciation: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The earthquake struck at the precise moment of deep sleep — ところに marks something unexpected arriving right at that specific moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女に電話をかけようとした{___}へ、彼女のほうからかかってきた。
([Just when] I had decided to give her a call, she phoned me.)
facts.explanation: ところに、ところへ、ところを ((Just) when) — 彼女に電話をかけようとしたところへ、彼女のほうからかかってきた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.reading: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.pronunciation: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Just as he decided to call her, she called first — ところへ marks a new event arriving right into the middle of the existing moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
犯人は、店から出てきた{___}、警官に捕らえられた。
([Just as] the thief left the store, a policeman arrested him.)
facts.explanation: ところに、ところへ、ところを ((Just) when) — 犯人は、店から出てきたところを、警官に捕らえられた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.reading: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.pronunciation: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The officer arrested him at the exact moment of leaving the store — ところを focuses on the precise action-in-progress that was caught or intercepted.

facts.quizQuestion:
お忙しい{___}来てくださって、ありがとうございます。
(Thank you very much for coming [when] you're so busy.)
facts.explanation: ところに、ところへ、ところを ((Just) when) — お忙しいところを来てくださって、ありがとうございます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.reading: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.pronunciation: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Thanking someone for coming despite being busy — ところを acknowledges they came at the specific moment when they were occupied, making the thanks more meaningful.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}お花の美しさ
(The [transient] beauty of flowers)
facts.explanation: 儚い (hopeless, fleeting, transient, fickle) — 儚いお花の美しさ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Flower beauty is the perfect subject — flowers bloom briefly and fade, which is exactly the fleeting, ephemeral quality described here.

facts.quizQuestion:
医者になる{___}。
(I [intended] to be a doctor.)
facts.explanation: 積もりだった (I had the intention to do (but I didn't)) — 医者になる積もりだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Becoming a doctor was the plan, but it didn't happen. 積もりだった looks back at a past intention that was never carried out.

facts.quizQuestion:
これはほんの冗談の{___}。
(This was [meant as] a joke.)
facts.explanation: 積もりだった (I had the intention to do (but I didn't)) — これはほんの冗談の積もりだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The joke was meant as a joke, not taken that way. 積もりだった describes the original intention behind an action.

facts.quizQuestion:
この夏ハワイへ行く{___}。
(I was [planning on] going to Hawaii this summer.)
facts.explanation: 積もりだった (I had the intention to do (but I didn't)) — この夏ハワイへ行く積もりだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The Hawaii trip was planned but didn't happen. 積もりだった reflects on what the speaker had intended before things fell through.

facts.quizQuestion:
僕は彼女と結婚するつ{___}。
(I [had the intention] to marry her (but I didn't).)
facts.explanation: 積もりだった (I had the intention to do (but I didn't)) — 僕は彼女と結婚するつもりだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He had intended to marry her — the plan existed — but he didn't follow through. つもりだった captures that gap between intention and outcome.

facts.quizQuestion:
今日は誰も来ないし、私も出かけないつ{___}。
(Today, nobody should have come, and I hadn't [intention] to leave too.)
facts.explanation: 積もりだった (I had the intention to do (but I didn't)) — 今日は誰も来ないし、私も出かけないつもりだった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Nobody was supposed to visit and the speaker wasn't planning to go out either. つもりだった reflects what was intended before the day unfolded.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女と結婚できる{___}、2、3年後になるだろう。
([Even if] I could marry her, it probably won't be for another two or three years.)
facts.explanation: としても (even if; assuming) — 彼女と結婚できるとしても、2、3年後になるだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Even if marrying her were possible, it still wouldn't happen soon — としても grants the hypothesis and shows the conclusion still holds.

facts.quizQuestion:
親が有名だ{___}、本人に実力がなければ社会で認められないでしょう。
([Even though] my parents are famous, society wouldn't have recognized their skills if they didn't really have them.)
facts.explanation: としても (even if; assuming) — 親が有名だとしても、本人に実力がなければ社会で認められないでしょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Even if parents are famous, personal ability is what matters — としても concedes the premise to argue that the outcome would be the same anyway.

facts.quizQuestion:
たとえ相手が部下だ{___}、世話になったらお礼の気持ちをちゃんと伝えるべきだ。
([Even if] it's a subordinate as your partner, you should properly thank them if they help you.)
facts.explanation: としても (even if; assuming) — たとえ相手が部下だとしても、世話になったらお礼の気持ちをちゃんと伝えるべきだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Even if the person helped you is a subordinate, you should thank them — としても concedes the condition to reinforce that the conclusion stands regardless.

facts.quizQuestion:
ゲーム用OS{___}進化を遂げつつあるLinux
(Advancing - [even as] an OS for game use : Linux.)
facts.explanation: としても (even if; assuming) — ゲーム用OSとしても進化を遂げつつあるLinux
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Even in the role of a gaming OS, Linux continues to evolve — としても concedes the framing to show the statement holds true even in that context.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼はとてもいい人だ{___}、私は彼を本当は信頼していない。
([Even if] he's very nice, I don't really trust him.)
facts.explanation: としても (even if; assuming) — 彼はとてもいい人だとしても、私は彼を本当は信頼していない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Even if he's a nice person, the speaker still doesn't trust him — としても concedes the good quality while the contrasting conclusion stands firm.

facts.quizQuestion:
雨がよく降ってる日が　家の中に遊び{___}です｡
(Its [easy to] play inside on rainy days.)
facts.explanation: やすい (Easy to V) — 雨がよく降ってる日が　家の中に遊びやすいです｡
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Identifies a condition that naturally makes an activity more convenient — rainy days lower the effort needed to stay inside and play.

facts.quizQuestion:
もしかしたら日本語で毎日、読んだり、書いたりしたら、日本語が分かり{___}くなると思います。
(If you do things like read and write in Japanese every day, Japanese will become [easy to] understand.)
facts.explanation: やすい (Easy to V) — もしかしたら日本語で毎日、読んだり、書いたりしたら、日本語が分かりやすくなると思います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes how daily practice lowers the barrier to understanding — Japanese becomes more accessible through regular effort.

facts.quizQuestion:
かいだんがぬれています。すべり{___}ので、ちゅういしてくたさい。
(The stairs are wet. They are slippery ([easy to] slip), so please be careful.)
facts.explanation: やすい (Easy to V) — かいだんがぬれています。すべりやすいので、ちゅういしてくたさい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Warns that wet stairs make slipping a likely outcome — the surface is the thing that makes the action easy to do.

facts.quizQuestion:
このくつはあのくつよりはき{___}です。
(These shoes here are [easier to] put on than the ones over there.)
facts.explanation: やすい (Easy to V) — このくつはあのくつよりはきやすいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Compares two pairs of shoes on how effortless they are to put on — one pair makes the action easier than the other.

facts.quizQuestion:
お金のために、愛してもいない男との生活をつづ{___}ならば、すっかり失望して、進退きわまる時が将来やってくることだろう。
(If she [continues to] live with a man she doesn't love for his money, the day will come when she will be utterly frustrated and between the devil and the deep blue sea.)
facts.explanation: 続ける (keep on doing ...) — お金のために、愛してもいない男との生活をつづけるならば、すっかり失望して、進退きわまる時が将来やってくることだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She keeps living that unfulfilling life without stopping — 続ける attaches to a verb stem to show the action continues without ceasing.

facts.quizQuestion:
この失敗、きっと一生、言われつづ{___}なあ。
(People will [keep] talking about this mistake forever.)
facts.explanation: 続ける (keep on doing ...) — この失敗、きっと一生、言われつづけるなあ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: People will go on talking about this mistake indefinitely — 続ける marks the talking as an action that keeps on going into the future.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は母国を愛しているし、これからも愛しつづ{___}だろう。
(I love, and will [continue to] love, my mother country.)
facts.explanation: 続ける (keep on doing ...) — 私は母国を愛しているし、これからも愛しつづけるだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: I have always loved and will continue to love my country — 続ける shows the action (loving) persists without interruption over time.

facts.quizQuestion:
無視しろぅ！　じゃ、書き{___}て！
(Ignore it! [Keep] writing!)
facts.explanation: 続ける (keep on doing ...) — 無視しろぅ！　じゃ、書き続けて！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The command is to keep writing without stopping — 続け is the て-form stem here, ordering someone to carry on with the action.

facts.quizQuestion:
「あばたもえくぼ」{___}言うからね。
(They [say] love is blind.)
facts.explanation: って (...said; no matter what) — 「あばたもえくぼ」って言うからね。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The proverb is being quoted or referenced — って marks it as something people say, a common expression being cited.

facts.quizQuestion:
「テレビの音を小さくしてもらえませんか」{___}言ったんですよ！
(I [said], "Could you please turn your television down?")
facts.explanation: って (...said; no matter what) — 「テレビの音を小さくしてもらえませんか」って言ったんですよ！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is reporting what they actually said in the past — って marks a direct or close quote of spoken words.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が探してた鍵{___}これ？
(Is this the key he was [looking for]?)
facts.explanation: って (...said; no matter what) — 彼が探してた鍵ってこれ？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Casually confirming whether this is the key he was looking for — って rephrases what was said or understood, asking for confirmation.

facts.quizQuestion:
知らない{___}
(He (She, They, etc.) [said] that he (she, they, etc.) did not know.)
facts.explanation: って (...said; no matter what) — 知らないって
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Reporting that someone said they didn't know — って here relays a statement, functioning like 'said that' or 'told me'.

facts.quizQuestion:
幾らたのまれた{___}できない
(I cannot do that [no matter how much] you insist)
facts.explanation: って (...said; no matter what) — 幾らたのまれたってできない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: No matter how much someone begs, the answer is no — って here means 'no matter what (is asked/said)'.

facts.quizQuestion:
シャンハイ（上海）は{___}寒くないです。
(The weather is [not very] cold in Shanghai.)
facts.explanation: あまり (not really) — シャンハイ（上海）はあまり寒くないです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes mild weather — Shanghai isn't freezing cold, just not particularly warm either.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は{___}きれいじゃない。
(She is [not very] pretty.)
facts.explanation: あまり (not really) — 彼女はあまりきれいじゃない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: A mild negative evaluation — the speaker isn't saying she's ugly, just that she's not especially pretty.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}優しくないです。
(He is [not very] nice.)
facts.explanation: あまり (not really) — 彼はあまり優しくないです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A restrained judgment about someone — he's not completely unkind, but he's not very nice either.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は{___}魚を食べません。
(She doesn't eat [a lot of] fish.)
facts.explanation: あまり (not really) — 彼女はあまり魚を食べません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes a low-frequency eating habit — she doesn't avoid fish entirely, she just doesn't eat a lot of it.

facts.quizQuestion:
桜大学は{___}有名な大学じゃありません。
(Sakura University is [not very] famous.)
facts.explanation: あまり (not really) — 桜大学はあまり有名な大学じゃありません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A gentle downgrade — Sakura University exists and functions, it's just not particularly well-known.

facts.quizQuestion:
結果{___}は採用しないことがあります。
([Depending upon] the outcome, you may not be hired.)
facts.explanation: によって (depending on) — 結果によっては採用しないことがあります。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The hiring decision hinges on the outcome — によって (depending on) shows the result varies conditional on that factor.

facts.quizQuestion:
天気{___}は中止することがあります。
([Depending upon] the weather, the event may be cancelled.)
facts.explanation: によって (depending on) — 天気によっては中止することがあります。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Whether the event is cancelled depends on the weather — によって marks weather as the variable factor the decision hinges on.

facts.quizQuestion:
景気によう{___}はメキシコに行きます。
([Depending upon] the finances, I will go to Mexico.)
facts.explanation: によって (depending on) — 景気にようってはメキシコに行きます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The question already has によう in it; the blank completes って to finish によって — it marks the financial situation as the deciding variable.

facts.quizQuestion:
漢字{___}意味が違います
([Depending on] kanji the meaning is different)
facts.explanation: によって (depending on) — 漢字によって意味が違います
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Kanji meaning varies depending on which character is used — によって marks the kanji as the varying factor that changes the meaning.

facts.quizQuestion:
天気{___}は来ます
([depending on] the weather, i will come)
facts.explanation: によって (depending on) — 天気によっては来ます
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Coming or not depends entirely on the weather — によって marks the variable condition that determines the outcome.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}二人は枯すすき、花の咲かない枯ススキ
([After all] we two are nothing but withered grass: flowerless grass)
facts.explanation: どうせ (after all; eventually; at best) — どうせ二人は枯すすき、花の咲かない枯ススキ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Sets a fatalistic, resigned tone — the speaker sees both of them as insignificant, looking at the bigger picture.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}郵便局へ行くのなら、切手を買ってくださいませんか？
(Since you're going to the post-office [anyway] won't you please buy some stamps for me?)
facts.explanation: どうせ (after all; eventually; at best) — どうせ郵便局へ行くのなら、切手を買ってくださいませんか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Captures a practical opportunity — since you're heading to the post office anyway, you might as well do both things.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}行かなければならないのなら早いほうがいいです。
(If you have to go [anyway], then you might as well do it quickly.)
facts.explanation: どうせ (after all; eventually; at best) — どうせ行かなければならないのなら早いほうがいいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Accepts the inevitable and makes the best of it — if you have to go regardless, doing it quickly is the sensible choice.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたが手伝ってくれないことは分かっている。{___}あなたはそういう人だ。
(I know you won't help me. That's the kind of guy you are, [after all].)
facts.explanation: どうせ (after all; eventually; at best) — あなたが手伝ってくれないことは分かっている。どうせあなたはそういう人だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Resigned acceptance of someone's nature — the speaker already knew the person wouldn't help, because that's just who they are.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}金なんてそのうちなくなるんだ
([After all] (eventually) money you now have will be gone.)
facts.explanation: どうせ (after all; eventually; at best) — どうせ金なんてそのうちなくなるんだ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Expresses the inevitability of money running out — no matter how much you have, it'll be gone eventually.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたの助けがなかったら、我々は仕事を間に合わせられなかった{___}。
(If it weren't for your help, we [probably] wouldn't have finished this job in time.)
facts.explanation: であろう (I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?) — あなたの助けがなかったら、我々は仕事を間に合わせられなかったであろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used in a formal hypothetical — without your help, it would have been very hard to finish on time (written register).

facts.quizQuestion:
おそらくそれは嘘{___}。
(I dare say it is [probably] a lie.)
facts.explanation: であろう (I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?) — おそらくそれは嘘であろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Formal way to state a firm belief — the speaker is quite confident this is a lie, stated in a literary tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
きっと彼女の病気はすぐによくなる{___}。
(That she will get over her illness soon is [certain].)
facts.explanation: であろう (I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?) — きっと彼女の病気はすぐによくなるであろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Expresses confident expectation in formal writing — the speaker is sure she will recover, stated with certainty.

facts.quizQuestion:
それはナンセンスだ。誰もそれを信じない{___}。
(That's nonsense. Nobody [would believe] it.)
facts.explanation: であろう (I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?) — それはナンセンスだ。誰もそれを信じないであろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used formally to state what is almost certain — nobody would believe nonsense like that, the speaker is sure of it.

facts.quizQuestion:
現在の私たちにとって、もっとも重要なのは、地球の環境を守ること{___}。自分と関係がないと考えずに、自分の問題として、この問題を考えることが求められている。
(I fear the more essential fact nowadays is the planet environment. If you don't think this have no relation with you, you are part of the problem, and you will [probably] be asked to think about this problem.)
facts.explanation: であろう (I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?) — 現在の私たちにとって、もっとも重要なのは、地球の環境を守ることであろう。自分と関係がないと考えずに、自分の問題として、この問題を考えることが求められている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Formal written assertion — in a persuasive essay, the writer states with conviction what they believe is most important today.

facts.quizQuestion:
注意しなかったので,病気{___}りました。
(Because I wasn't careful, I [became] sick.)
facts.explanation: になる (To become) — 注意しなかったので,病気になりました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Not taking care led to a state change — becoming sick is the natural result of a situation, expressed by になる.

facts.quizQuestion:
ざっと計算しても大変なこと{___}！
(Even by a rough calculation we are going [to be] in a terrible state!)
facts.explanation: になる (To become) — ざっと計算しても大変なことになる！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A rough calculation reveals the situation will become terrible — になる describes the state things are heading toward.

facts.quizQuestion:
将来には先生{___}りたいです。
(In the future, I want [to become] a teacher.)
facts.explanation: になる (To become) — 将来には先生になりたいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Wanting to become a teacher is a future state the speaker aims for — になる expresses that desired transformation.

facts.quizQuestion:
いい{___}よ。
(That's just fine[, I tell you].)
facts.explanation: わ ((adds emphasis) [female]) — いいわよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A mild, casual female affirmation — わ softens the agreement while still expressing genuine feeling.

facts.quizQuestion:
この本おもしろい{___}ね。
(This book is interesting, isn't it[!] [F])
facts.explanation: わ ((adds emphasis) [female]) — この本おもしろいわね。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Sharing an impression about the book with the listener — わ adds a soft, gentle emphasis typical of female casual speech.

facts.quizQuestion:
(レジで)あら、お金が足りない{___}。困った。このにんじんは買えません。
((At the cash register) oh, I don't have enough money[, you know]. I'm in trouble. I can't buy this carrot.)
facts.explanation: わ ((adds emphasis) [female]) — (レジで)あら、どうしよう。お財布にお金が入ってないわ。困ったわね。このにんじんは要らないわ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is expressing mild distress and resignation at the cash register — わ gives the exclamations that soft, personal female tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
私が子供の頃、日本という国{___}知っていたことは、東アジアの島国だということぐらいでした。（here ぐらい expresses the aproximation)
(In my childhood, all I knew [about] Japan was that it is an insular country situated in Eastern Asia.)
facts.explanation: について (regarding, about) — 私が子供の頃、日本という国について知っていたことは、東アジアの島国だということぐらいでした。（here ぐらい expresses the aproximation)
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Knowing facts about Japan is the topic being reflected on — について introduces the subject matter under discussion or consideration.

facts.quizQuestion:
晩餐会の席でソトウェアの新バージョンの発{___}触れたい。
(I want to mention [touch upon] the release of our new software edition at the banquet.)
facts.explanation: について (regarding, about) — 晩餐会の席でソトウェアの新バージョンの発について触れたい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The new software release is the topic to be touched on — について marks what the speaker intends to address at the banquet.

facts.quizQuestion:
計画{___}
([regarding] the plan)
facts.explanation: について (regarding, about) — 計画について
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The plan is the subject being referred to — について attaches to a noun to signal it as the topic of discussion or inquiry.

facts.quizQuestion:
私が行くこと{___}
([regarding] my going there)
facts.explanation: について (regarding, about) — 私が行くことについて
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Going there is the topic under consideration — について marks the act of going as the subject matter being discussed.

facts.quizQuestion:
コンピューターの使い方{___}の本がほしい。
(I want a book [regarding] the usage of computers.)
facts.explanation: について (regarding, about) — コンピューターの使い方についての本がほしい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Computer usage is the topic the desired book would cover — について marks the subject matter the book should be about.

facts.quizQuestion:
雨が降ってきました。そ{___}悪いことに強い風が吹いてきました。
(It began rain. [To make matters worse], a strong wind was blowing.)
facts.explanation: 其の上 (moreover; besides) — 雨が降ってきました。その上悪いことに強い風が吹いてきました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Rain started, and on top of that strong wind arrived — その上 adds the second bad thing piling on top of the first.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は英語を話せる。そ{___}、フランス語も話せる。
(He can speak English. [In addition], he can speak French.)
facts.explanation: 其の上 (moreover; besides) — 彼は英語を話せる。その上、フランス語も話せる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He speaks English, and additionally French — その上 adds the second language as an extra on top of what was already stated.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}、あなたは社長ですか？
(Are you the president, [by any chance]?)
facts.explanation: もしかして (is it possible) — もしかして、あなたは社長ですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Softens a direct question by adding uncertainty — you're not sure if this person is actually the president, so you hedge.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}名案じゃないかな。
([Maybe] it's a great idea.)
facts.explanation: もしかして (is it possible) — もしかして名案じゃないかな。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Expresses a tentative positive possibility — maybe this really is a great idea, though you're not committing to it.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}財布を落としたかも。
([Maybe] I dropped down my wallet.)
facts.explanation: もしかして (is it possible) — もしかして財布を落としたかも。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Raises an uncertain possibility about yourself — you have a nagging feeling you might have dropped your wallet.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}鍵を失くしたのですか？
(Do you [happen to] lose your key?)
facts.explanation: もしかして (is it possible) — もしかして鍵を失くしたのですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used to ask about something uncertain — you noticed something off and want to check if they actually lost their key.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなた、{___}、俳優？
(Are you, [by any chance], an actor?)
facts.explanation: もしかして (is it possible) — あなた、もしかして、俳優？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Poses a tentative guess as a question — you think this person might be an actor, but you want to check before assuming.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は宿題をし{___}。
(I [must] do my assignment.)
facts.explanation: なければならない (must) — 私は宿題をしなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Homework is something that must get done. なければならない states this as a plain, neutral obligation in everyday speech.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は日本語を勉強し{___}。
(I [have to] study Japanese.)
facts.explanation: なければならない (must) — 私は日本語を勉強しなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Studying Japanese is framed as something the speaker has to do — a personal obligation stated plainly.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は今週、期末試験を受け{___}。
(I [have to] take my final exams this week.)
facts.explanation: なければならない (must) — 私は今週、期末試験を受けなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Final exams are unavoidable this week. なければならない expresses this as a necessary, non-optional obligation.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は朝食を食べ{___}。
(I [have to] eat breakfast.)
facts.explanation: なければならない (must) — 私は朝食を食べなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Breakfast is framed as something the speaker has to eat — a regular, necessary obligation stated simply.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は10時ごろに出発し{___}。
(I [have to] get going at about 10)
facts.explanation: なければならない (must) — 私は10時ごろに出発しなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker needs to leave around 10. なければならない states this as a time-bound obligation.

facts.quizQuestion:
今夜は眠れ{___}。
(I don't think I [can] sleep tonight.)
facts.explanation: そうもない (no way; impossible) — 今夜は眠れそうもない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: States that something seems impossible from your current perspective — the speaker feels sleep tonight is just not going to happen.

facts.quizQuestion:
明日は雪が降り{___}。
(There is [no way] it snows tomorrow.)
facts.explanation: そうもない (no way; impossible) — 明日は雪が降りそうもない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Strongly rules out a possibility — based on what you see, there's no chance it will snow tomorrow.

facts.quizQuestion:
明日までにこの宿題を終われ{___}。
(I don't think I [can] finish this homework by tomorrow.)
facts.explanation: そうもない (no way; impossible) — 明日までにこの宿題を終われそうもない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Expresses a feeling that something won't realistically happen — finishing all that homework by tomorrow feels out of reach.

facts.quizQuestion:
あまり期待でき{___}。
(There is [not much hope].)
facts.explanation: そうもない (no way; impossible) — あまり期待できそうもない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Conveys a low level of hope — there just doesn't seem to be much expectation that things will improve.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは一度その映画を見たら、忘れ{___}でしょう。
(Once you see the movie, you will [never] forget it.)
facts.explanation: そうもない (no way; impossible) — あなたは一度その映画を見たら、忘れそうもないでしょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: Strongly implies something is unforgettable — once you see that movie, forgetting it will be basically impossible.

facts.quizQuestion:
謝りなさい。{___}、怒りますよ。
(Say you're sorry right now. [Otherwise], I will be angry.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (or else, otherwise) — 謝りなさい。さもないと、怒りますよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The consequence of not apologizing is anger — さもないと warns that the bad outcome will happen if the action isn't taken.

facts.quizQuestion:
このバスに乗らなくてはいけない、{___}学校に遅刻してしまう。
(I have to get on this bus, [otherwise] I'll be late for school.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (or else, otherwise) — このバスに乗らなくてはいけない、さもないと学校に遅刻してしまう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Missing the bus is the consequence of not getting on — さもないと connects the required action to its negative alternative.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は急がなければなりません、{___}、バスに遅れます。
(I have to hurry up, [otherwise] I'll miss the bus.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (or else, otherwise) — 私は急がなければなりません、さもないと、バスに遅れます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Missing the bus follows from not hurrying — さもないと makes the warning explicit: do it, or face the consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
ボタンを留めなさい。{___}、風邪をひきますよ。
(Button up your coat, [otherwise] you will catch cold.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (or else, otherwise) — ボタンを留めなさい。さもないと、風邪をひきますよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Not buttoning up will lead to catching cold — さもないと states the warning plainly after the instruction.

facts.quizQuestion:
英語を勉強しなさい、{___}テストに落ちますよ。
(Study English, [otherwise] you will fail the test.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (or else, otherwise) — 英語を勉強しなさい、さもないとテストに落ちますよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Failing the test is what happens if you don't study — さもないと links the command to the consequence of ignoring it.

facts.quizQuestion:
コーラ{___}おごるよ。
(Let me buy you a coke [or something].)
facts.explanation: か何か (or something like that) — コーラか何かおごるよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker wants to treat someone but isn't locked into a specific drink — a coke or whatever else sounds good.

facts.quizQuestion:
そこには猿{___}います。
(There are monkeys [or something] there.)
facts.explanation: か何か (or something like that) — そこには猿か何かいます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker saw something animal-like but isn't certain what — monkeys, or something of that sort.

facts.quizQuestion:
冷たい飲み物{___}ください。
(Please give me a cool drink [or something].)
facts.explanation: か何か (or something like that) — 冷たい飲み物か何かください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The request is open-ended — a cold drink, whatever kind is available, would be welcome.

facts.quizQuestion:
野球{___}したいです。
(I want to play baseball [or something].)
facts.explanation: か何か (or something like that) — 野球か何かしたいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The desire is for some kind of sport or activity, with baseball as the vague example rather than a firm demand.

facts.quizQuestion:
あれはそんなによかっ{___}？
([Was it] that good?)
facts.explanation: ~たっけ (was it~; did you say~) — あれはそんなによかったっけ？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is recalling a past impression and asking whether they remember it correctly — a soft, uncertain check on memory.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつ映画を見にいっ{___}？
(When [did we go] to a movie?)
facts.explanation: ~たっけ (was it~; did you say~) — いつ映画を見にいったっけ？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker can't quite place when it was. たっけ turns the question into a gentle memory search rather than a direct query.

facts.quizQuestion:
どこまで話し{___}？
(Where [did I leave off]?)
facts.explanation: ~たっけ (was it~; did you say~) — どこまで話したっけ？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker lost track of where they stopped. たっけ signals they're trying to retrieve that point from memory.

facts.quizQuestion:
ジョンの家はどこでし{___}？
(Where is John's house [again]?)
facts.explanation: ~たっけ (was it~; did you say~) — ジョンの家はどこでしたっけ？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is asking someone else to remind them — they've forgotten John's address and たっけ softens the question with a 'wasn't it…?' tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
お昼ご飯何食べ{___}？
(What [did I eat] lunch today?)
facts.explanation: ~たっけ (was it~; did you say~) — お昼ご飯何食べたっけ？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker genuinely can't remember what they ate. たっけ is used for self-directed memory searches like this.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼はとてもあなた{___}に見える。
(He [looks just like] you.)
facts.explanation: の様 (seems like; looks like) — 彼はとてもあなたの様に見える。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Used in formal/written style to describe a visual resemblance — he looks just like you, in the author's observation.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼はまるで王{___}だった。
(He [looked like] a king.)
facts.explanation: の様 (seems like; looks like) — 彼はまるで王の様だった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Formal comparison to appearance — the way he carried himself gave the strong impression of royalty.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼はお疲れ{___}だ。
(He [looks] tired.)
facts.explanation: の様 (seems like; looks like) — 彼はお疲れの様だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Formal observation about how someone looks — based on his appearance, he seems to be tired.

facts.quizQuestion:
鬼{___}な先生だ。
(A teacher [look] a devil.)
facts.explanation: の様 (seems like; looks like) — 鬼の様な先生だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes someone's demeanor in formal terms — the teacher's manner is as frightening as a demon's.

facts.quizQuestion:
わたしに飲み物を{___}？
([Please] give me something to drink?)
facts.explanation: 下さいませんか (kudaisamasenka) — わたしに飲み物を下さいませんか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Asking someone to give you a drink in a polite, slightly formal way. くださいませんか adds a soft, requesting tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
英語で自己紹介して{___}？
([Could you] tell me about yourself in English?)
facts.explanation: 下さいませんか (kudaisamasenka) — 英語で自己紹介して下さいませんか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Politely asking someone to introduce themselves in English. くださいませんか makes the request courteous rather than direct.

facts.quizQuestion:
その本を貸して{___}？
([Could you] lend me the book?)
facts.explanation: 下さいませんか (kudaisamasenka) — その本を貸して下さいませんか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Asking to borrow a book in a considerate, polite way. くださいませんか softens the request into a gentle question.

facts.quizQuestion:
お代わりを{___}？
([Could you] give me another one?)
facts.explanation: 下さいませんか (kudaisamasenka) — お代わりを下さいませんか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Politely asking for another serving. くださいませんか frames it as a courteous, tentative request.

facts.quizQuestion:
もっとはっきり説明して{___}？
([Would you] explain that more clearly?)
facts.explanation: 下さいませんか (kudaisamasenka) — もっとはっきり説明して下さいませんか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Asking someone to explain more clearly, phrased as a polite request rather than a demand.

facts.quizQuestion:
このペンをお借りし{___}？
([May I] borrow this pen?)
facts.explanation: ~てもいいですか (do you mind if~) — このペンをお借りしてもいいですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Politely asks the other person if it's acceptable to borrow something — phrased as a question to seek permission.

facts.quizQuestion:
家に帰っ{___}？
([May I] go back home?)
facts.explanation: ~てもいいですか (do you mind if~) — 家に帰ってもいいですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Checks whether it's okay to leave and go home — a polite request for permission to do something the speaker wants.

facts.quizQuestion:
机の上のりんごを食べ{___}？
([May I] eat these apples on the table?)
facts.explanation: ~てもいいですか (do you mind if~) — 机の上のりんごを食べてもいいですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: Asks if taking the apples would be acceptable — the speaker wants to eat them but is checking first.

facts.quizQuestion:
ここに座っ{___}？
([Do you mind if] I sit here?)
facts.explanation: ~てもいいですか (do you mind if~) — ここに座ってもいいですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Seeks consent before sitting in a spot that might be taken — a considerate way to ask if an action is permitted.

facts.quizQuestion:
先生に話し{___}？
([Can I] talk to my teacher?)
facts.explanation: ~てもいいですか (do you mind if~) — 先生に話してもいいですか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Politely asks permission to initiate a conversation with the teacher — turning the action into a question first.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}お座りください。
([Please] sit down.)
facts.explanation: どうぞ (go ahead; be my guest) — どうぞお座りください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is inviting someone to sit down warmly. どうぞ adds a welcoming, 'please go right ahead' feeling to the invitation.

facts.quizQuestion:
スナック菓子をご自由に{___}。
([Help yourself] to a snack.)
facts.explanation: どうぞ (go ahead; be my guest) — スナック菓子をご自由にどうぞ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is offering snacks freely and encouraging the listener to help themselves. どうぞ signals open permission.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}座ったままで結構です。
([Please] keep your seats.)
facts.explanation: どうぞ (go ahead; be my guest) — どうぞ座ったままで結構です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is asking people to stay seated — どうぞ here softens the instruction into a warm, polite gesture.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}ごゆっくりしていってください。
(You [can] stay as long as you like.)
facts.explanation: どうぞ (go ahead; be my guest) — どうぞごゆっくりしていってください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is inviting someone to stay and relax without hurry. どうぞ adds a generous, welcoming tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}、おっしゃってください。
([Go ahead] and say it.)
facts.explanation: どうぞ (go ahead; be my guest) — どうぞ、おっしゃってください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is giving someone permission to go ahead and speak. どうぞ signals 'you have the floor — please proceed.'

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}のも忘れちゃったよ
(I lost track of [time].)
facts.explanation: 時間がたつ (passing of time;) — 時間がたつのも忘れちゃったよ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: They forgot that time was passing — 時間がたつ is the phrase for time passing, and here it's what the speaker lost track of.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は{___}のを待った。
(I waited for [time to pass].)
facts.explanation: 時間がたつ (passing of time;) — 私は時間がたつのを待った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Actively waiting for time to pass — 時間がたつ is what you wait for when you're just letting time go by.

facts.quizQuestion:
忙しいときは{___}のが早い。
([Time passes] quickly when you're busy.)
facts.explanation: 時間がたつ (passing of time;) — 忙しいときは時間がたつのが早い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Time passing quickly is the subject of the sentence — 時間がたつ is the expression used when commenting on how fast or slow time goes.

facts.quizQuestion:
楽しいとき、{___}のは早い。
([Time flies] when you are having fun.)
facts.explanation: 時間がたつ (passing of time;) — 楽しいとき、時間がたつのは早い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Time flying when having fun is a common observation about how time passes — 時間がたつ is the phrase that captures this passage of time.

facts.quizQuestion:
コンデンサは{___}と電荷を失う。
(Capacitors lose their charge [over time].)
facts.explanation: 時間がたつ (passing of time;) — コンデンサは時間がたつと電荷を失う。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: As time passes, capacitors lose charge — 時間がたつ describes the natural process of time going by that causes the change.

facts.quizQuestion:
会議中（{___}）にお腹がグーグー鳴っていて困ったよ。
(My stomach started growling right there [in the meeting]. It was embarrassing.)
facts.explanation: じゅう／ちゅう (throughout, through, during all) — 会議中（ちゅう）にお腹がグーグー鳴っていて困ったよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The stomach growling happened throughout the meeting — ちゅう attaches to 会議 to mean 'during the entire meeting,' showing the time span.

facts.quizQuestion:
家内は海外旅行中（{___}）よく電話をかけてきた。
(My wife often rang me up [during] her travel abroad.)
facts.explanation: じゅう／ちゅう (throughout, through, during all) — 家内は海外旅行中（ちゅう）よく電話をかけてきた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The calls happened repeatedly during the whole overseas trip — ちゅう expresses the entire duration of travel abroad as the time frame.

facts.quizQuestion:
上例の{___}使えば、いいと思います。
(I think it is alright if you use it [like] the example above.)
facts.explanation: ように (in order to) — 上例のように使えば、いいと思います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Using it like the example is the suggested approach. ように expresses 'in the manner of' — follow the example as your target.

facts.quizQuestion:
忘れ物をしない{___}ご注意して下さい。
([In order not to] leave things, please take care (often heard on trains).)
facts.explanation: ように (in order to) — 忘れ物をしないようにご注意して下さい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Taking care is the desired behavior to prevent forgetting. ように connects the caution to the goal of not leaving things behind.

facts.quizQuestion:
この度の流出したデータを入手された方はお手数ですが削除いただけます{___}お願い申し上げます。
(Please excuse us for the bother, but could those people who have acquired the data erroneously disseminated on this occasion please delete them.)
facts.explanation: ように (in order to) — この度の流出したデータを入手された方はお手数ですが削除いただけますようお願い申し上げます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
