facts.grammarNote: The request is for people to delete the data. ように here frames a polite indirect instruction — 'please do it so that this happens.'

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたが元気になります{___}
(I pray [you get better soon].)
facts.explanation: ように (in order to) — あなたが元気になりますように
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is wishing for someone's recovery. ように here expresses a hope or prayer directed at a desired future state.

facts.quizQuestion:
パソコン管理が煩雑になってきましたので、管理しやすい{___}徐々に変更していきます。
(The computer admin has become really tricky, so [in order to] make it easier to do, I'll be changing things bit by bit.)
facts.explanation: ように (in order to) — パソコン管理が煩雑になってきましたので、管理しやすいように徐々に変更していきます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Making things easier to manage is the goal. ように sets up the target state that the coming changes are designed to achieve.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}早く参ります。
(I'll come [as soon as I can].)
facts.explanation: できるだけ (as soon/early as possible) — できるだけ早く参ります。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Commits to arriving promptly — the speaker will come as early as their situation allows.

facts.quizQuestion:
この本は{___}早く返してくれ。
(Return this book [as soon as you can].)
facts.explanation: できるだけ (as soon/early as possible) — この本はできるだけ早く返してくれ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A polite but firm request — please return the book at your earliest opportunity.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}早く起きなさい。
(Get up [as early as you can].)
facts.explanation: できるだけ (as soon/early as possible) — できるだけ早く起きなさい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Sets the target as the earliest possible wake-up — wake up as early as you can manage.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}速く走ったので、友人に追いつけた。
(Running [as fast as I could], I was able to catch up with my friend.)
facts.explanation: できるだけ (as soon/early as possible) — できるだけ速く走ったので、友人に追いつけた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speed was at its maximum — running as fast as possible was what made catching up achievable.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}長く生きたいと思っている。
(He wants to live [as long as he can].)
facts.explanation: できるだけ (as soon/early as possible) — 彼はできるだけ長く生きたいと思っている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A life goal expressed at maximum extent — he wants to live for as long as he possibly can.

facts.quizQuestion:
関係者は真実を明らかに{___}
(Those involved [must] tell the truth.)
facts.explanation: せねばならない (Have to, feel obliged to) — 関係者は真実を明らかにせねばならない
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Those involved bear a responsibility to come clean. せねばならない gives this a formal, weighty tone fitting a serious obligation.

facts.quizQuestion:
これらの製品の新たなる販路を開拓{___}。
(We [have to] find a new market for these products.)
facts.explanation: せねばならない (Have to, feel obliged to) — これらの製品の新たなる販路を開拓せねばならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Finding new markets is a business necessity. せねばならない frames this as a pressing formal duty the team must fulfill.

facts.quizQuestion:
是非とも目標を達成{___}。
(I [have to] attain my purpose at all costs.)
facts.explanation: せねばならない (Have to, feel obliged to) — 是非とも目標を達成せねばならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The goal must be reached no matter what. せねばならない adds a determined, serious tone to this obligation.

facts.quizQuestion:
老人を尊敬{___}。
(You [have to] respect the old.)
facts.explanation: せねばならない (Have to, feel obliged to) — 老人を尊敬せねばならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Respecting elders is framed as a moral duty. せねばならない gives this principle a formal, principled weight.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は彼に遅れをとらないように一生懸命勉強{___}。
(I [must] study hard in order to keep up of him.)
facts.explanation: せねばならない (Have to, feel obliged to) — 私は彼に遅れをとらないように一生懸命勉強せねばならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: Keeping up with him requires hard work. せねばならない expresses the effort as a firm and necessary commitment.

facts.quizQuestion:
それを自分に対して、{___}他人に対して認めるのは、とても難しいことだ。
(It's very hard to admit that to yourself [let alone] to other people.)
facts.explanation: ましてや (Let alone, still, much less) — それを自分に対して、ましてや他人に対して認めるのは、とても難しいことだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Admitting something to yourself is hard; admitting it to others is even harder — ましてや escalates from the first to the more extreme case.

facts.quizQuestion:
私はギターを弾くことはできない。{___}バイオリンは弾けない。
(I can't play the guitar, [much less] the violin.)
facts.explanation: ましてや (Let alone, still, much less) — 私はギターを弾くことはできない。ましてやバイオリンは弾けない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Can't play guitar, and violin is an even bigger stretch — ましてや escalates to the more difficult instrument.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は誰にもこの時計を貸すつもりはありません。{___}手放すつもりなど毛頭ありません。
(I have no intention of lending this watch to anyone, [much less] parting with it.)
facts.explanation: ましてや (Let alone, still, much less) — 私は誰にもこの時計を貸すつもりはありません。ましてや手放すつもりなど毛頭ありません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Won't even lend the watch, let alone give it away — ましてや takes an already firm position and escalates it further.

facts.quizQuestion:
私は中古車を買う余裕などなく、{___}新車などはもってのほかだ。
(I can't afford to buy a used car, [much less] a new car.)
facts.explanation: ましてや (Let alone, still, much less) — 私は中古車を買う余裕などなく、ましてや新車などはもってのほかだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Can't afford a used car, and a new one is completely out of the question — ましてや escalates to the more extreme option.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は、フランス語を話せる、{___}英語も話せる。
(He can speak French, [still more] English.)
facts.explanation: ましてや (Let alone, still, much less) — 彼は、フランス語を話せる、ましてや英語も話せる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Already speaks French, and English is even more within reach — ましてや here escalates positively to the even stronger case.

facts.quizQuestion:
レンズ{___}町を眺める。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Viewing the town happens via the lens as an intermediary medium — を通して marks the lens as the channel or instrument of perception.

facts.quizQuestion:
この目{___}見た現実。
(Reality as seen [through one's own eyes].)
facts.explanation: を通して (through, by use of, by means of) — この目を通して見た現実。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Reality is perceived by passing through one's own eyes — を通して marks the eyes as the medium of experience.

facts.quizQuestion:
紹介者{___}発注する。
(Place an order [through] an intermediary.)
facts.explanation: を通して (through, by use of, by means of) — 紹介者を通して発注する。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The order is placed by routing it through an intermediary — を通して marks the go-between as the channel the transaction passes through.

facts.quizQuestion:
通訳{___}会談する。
(Hold discussions [through] an interpreter.)
facts.explanation: を通して (through, by use of, by means of) — 通訳を通して会談する。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The discussions are conducted using the interpreter as the communication channel — を通して marks the interpreter as the medium connecting both sides.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女の評判は田中さん{___}知っている。
(I know of her reputation [via] Mrs. Tanaka.)
facts.explanation: を通して (through, by use of, by means of) — 彼女の評判は田中さんを通して知っている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The information about her reputation came via Mrs. Tanaka as a source — を通して marks her as the intermediary channel through which it was learned.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたはたくさんお金をもっているが、私は少しも（お金を）持って{___}。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The contrast with 'a lot' makes total absence clear — you don't have even the smallest amount of money.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは少しも幸せでは{___}。
(You are [not in the least] happy.)
facts.explanation: 少しも〜ない (not at all) — あなたは少しも幸せではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Reinforces complete unhappiness — not even a tiny bit of happiness exists in this person's situation.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは少しも眠れ{___}ことがある。
(Sometimes you can't sleep [at all].)
facts.explanation: 少しも〜ない (not at all) — あなたは少しも眠れないことがある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes total sleeplessness when it happens — on those nights, not even a little sleep is possible.

facts.quizQuestion:
いいえ、{___}（ありません）。
([Not at all].)
facts.explanation: 少しも〜ない (not at all) — いいえ、少しも（ありません）。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A polite, complete denial — 'not at all' is the full answer, rejecting the premise entirely.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}気になるものだ
([precisely] what is so worrisome.)
facts.explanation: だからこそ ((just) because of) — だからこそ気になるものだ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The worrisome quality is the precise cause — it's the fact that this is worrying that makes it keep nagging at you.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}私は本当に〜したい。
(and that's [exactly why] I would really like to...)
facts.explanation: だからこそ ((just) because of) — だからこそ私は本当に〜したい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Emphasizes a personal motivation — it is specifically because of this reason that the speaker wants to do something.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}注意しないといけないんだよ。
(All the more [reason] to be careful.)
facts.explanation: だからこそ ((just) because of) — だからこそ注意しないといけないんだよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Strengthens the case for caution — the very fact that this situation exists is all the more reason to be careful.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたが来いと言った{___}私は来たんです。
(I came [especially because] you said to.)
facts.explanation: だからこそ ((just) because of) — あなたが来いと言ったからこそ私は来たんです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Pinpoints the reason for coming — the speaker came specifically and only because you told them to.

facts.quizQuestion:
本当にいくの? 行く{___}！
(Will you really go there? [I said] I would go!)
facts.explanation: ってば (emphasize speakers emotion/desire/will) — 本当にいくの? 行くってば！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is repeating their stated intention with emotion after being doubted — ってば insists on what was already said.

facts.quizQuestion:
早く帰りなさい{___}！
([I told you] go home at once!)
facts.explanation: ってば (emphasize speakers emotion/desire/will) — 早く帰りなさいってば！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The instruction to go home is being repeated with clear frustration — ってば shows the speaker is fed up with not being heard.

facts.quizQuestion:
何だ{___}！
([I say] what's that!)
facts.explanation: ってば (emphasize speakers emotion/desire/will) — 何だってば！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: An emphatic, emotionally charged exclamation — ってば here adds a sharp insistence to the question.

facts.quizQuestion:
マールでいい{___}！
([I'm telling you], Marle's fine!)
facts.explanation: ってば (emphasize speakers emotion/desire/will) — マールでいいってば！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is insisting that Marle is fine, probably after being challenged — ってば conveys 'I already told you this!'.

facts.quizQuestion:
昔は新婚旅行といえばハワイだったが、{___}トルコやエジプトも珍しくない。
(Long ago, if mentioning honeymoon travel, you would think of Hawaii, but [now] Turkey and Egypt are also not unusual.)
facts.explanation: 今では ("(by) now"; "nowadays") — 昔は新婚旅行といえばハワイだったが、今ではトルコやエジプトも珍しくない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Signals that things have changed from how they used to be — Hawaii was once the honeymoon default, but that's no longer true today.

facts.quizQuestion:
おじは{___}安楽に暮らしている。
(My uncle [now] lives in comfort.)
facts.explanation: 今では ("(by) now"; "nowadays") — おじは今では安楽に暮らしている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Implies a contrast with the past — your uncle's situation has shifted, and he now enjoys a comfortable life.

facts.quizQuestion:
この型のカメラは{___}流行遅れである。
(This type of camera is [now] out of fashion.)
facts.explanation: 今では ("(by) now"; "nowadays") — この型のカメラは今では流行遅れである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Marks a change from what was once current — this camera style was fashionable before, but that era has passed.

facts.quizQuestion:
この言葉は{___}用いられない。
(This word has gone out of use [now].)
facts.explanation: 今では ("(by) now"; "nowadays") — この言葉は今では用いられない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Shows that something that once existed no longer does — the word was used at some point, but not anymore.

facts.quizQuestion:
久し{___}に田舎へ帰りました。
(After [a long time] I returned back to my hometown.)
facts.explanation: ~ぶり (first time in ~) — 久しぶりに田舎へ帰りました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Returning home after a long absence — ぶり marks the time span that passed since the last visit.

facts.quizQuestion:
３年{___}に日本に帰ってきました！
(I'm back to Japan [after 3 years]!)
facts.explanation: ~ぶり (first time in ~) — ３年ぶりに日本に帰ってきました！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Coming back to Japan after a three-year gap — ぶり attaches to the time span to say this is the first time in that period.

facts.quizQuestion:
どちらかと言{___}海に行きたい。
([If I had my druthers], I would like to go to the sea.)
facts.explanation: どちらかといえば (If I had to say...) — どちらかと言えば海に行きたい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Sets up a conditional preference — 'if I had to choose' leads to the speaker's actual leaning toward the sea.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}泳ぎはうまい方でした。
(He was, [if anything], a good swimmer.)
facts.explanation: どちらかといえば (If I had to say...) — どちらかといえば泳ぎはうまい方でした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Acknowledges a slight tendency — if you were forced to assess him, he was more on the good-swimmer side.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}今日はあの人に会いたくない。
(I [would rather] not see him(her) today.)
facts.explanation: どちらかといえば (If I had to say...) — どちらかといえば今日はあの人に会いたくない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: States a mild preference in the negative — given a choice, the speaker would lean toward not seeing this person today.

facts.quizQuestion:
したい{___}にする
(Do [as you like].)
facts.explanation: 放題 (As you like) — したい放題にする
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Removes all limits — you can do whatever you want, as much as you want, without restriction.

facts.quizQuestion:
飲み{___}
([all you can] drink.)
facts.explanation: 放題 (As you like) — 飲み放題
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A common phrase meaning there's no limit on how much you can drink — common at all-inclusive events.

facts.quizQuestion:
食べ{___}
([all you can] eat.)
facts.explanation: 放題 (As you like) — 食べ放題
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Removes limits on eating — you can eat as much as you like, which is the whole appeal of an all-you-can-eat deal.

facts.quizQuestion:
第一回目試験を落第したから、受け{___}つもりだよ。dai ikkai me shiken wo rakudai shita kara, uke-naosu tsumori da yo.
(Since (I) failed the test the first time, (I'm) planning on [re-taking] it.)
facts.explanation: ~直す (to do something again, hoping to improve it) — 第一回目試験を落第したから、受け直すつもりだよ。dai ikkai me shiken wo rakudai shita kara, uke-naosu tsumori da yo.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Failing the first time means retaking the exam is doing the same action over again — 直す means to redo or repeat an action, often to correct a previous result.

facts.quizQuestion:
９時に彼女らと会うのか？{___}ビールを飲む時間はないんだよ！ くそ！
(We're meeting the girls at 9? [That means] there's no time to drink beer! Damnit!)
facts.explanation: そうすると (That means, If that's the case, etc) — ９時に彼女らと会うのか？そうするとビールを飲む時間はないんだよ！ くそ！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Learning there's a 9 o'clock meeting, the speaker draws an immediate conclusion: no time for beer — そうすると reasons from new information.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}いやが上にも美しく見える。
([That makes] you much more attractive still.)
facts.explanation: そうすると (That means, If that's the case, etc) — そうするといやが上にも美しく見える。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Given the situation just described, that outcome follows — そうすると draws a conclusion based on the preceding context.

facts.quizQuestion:
あの人は{___}母を想いださせる。
(She [vaguely] resembles my mother.)
facts.explanation: なんとなく (Vaguely, somehow) — あの人はなんとなく母を想いださせる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The resemblance is unclear and hard to pin down — she doesn't definitively look like the speaker's mother, but there's something.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}京都に行きたくなった。
([Somehow] I wanted to go to Kyoto.)
facts.explanation: なんとなく (Vaguely, somehow) — なんとなく京都に行きたくなった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A desire without a clear reason — the speaker felt the pull to go to Kyoto but couldn't explain why.

facts.quizQuestion:
日本のソフトクリームとは、ラベンダー{___}様々な珍しい味が楽しめる商品だ。
(Japanese soft-serve ice cream is a product you can enjoy in a variety of exotic flavors, [such as] Lavender.)
facts.explanation: ひとつとっても (One example being, for example) — 日本のソフトクリームとは、ラベンダーひとつとっても様々な珍しい味が楽しめる商品だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Uses lavender as one specific example to illustrate the variety — just take lavender as a single case among many.

facts.quizQuestion:
部屋探し{___}外国人として日本に生活を始めるのが難しいと言われる。
(It is said that it is hard to start living in Japan as a foreigner; [take the search for an apartment as an example].)
facts.explanation: ひとつとっても (One example being, for example) — 部屋探しひとつとっても外国人として日本に生活を始めるのが難しいと言われる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Uses apartment hunting as a concrete illustration — out of all the challenges, this one example alone shows how hard it is.

facts.quizQuestion:
今日の天気が昨日のような天気だったら良かった。{___}
(I [wish] today's weather had been as nice as yesterday's.)
facts.explanation: のに (wish) — 今日の天気が昨日のような天気だったら良かった。のに
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: If only today's weather had been as nice — のに at the end of a sentence expresses a wish or longing for an unrealized situation.

facts.quizQuestion:
雨さえやめばいい{___}なあ。
([If only] it would stop raining!)
facts.explanation: のに (wish) — 雨さえやめばいいのになあ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: If only the rain would stop — のに expresses a heartfelt wish for something to be different from the current situation.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは{___}とても若い。
(you are [still] very young.)
facts.explanation: 未だ (yet / as yet / so far/ not yet) — あなたは未だとても若い。未だ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Emphasizes ongoing youth — you haven't aged out of anything yet, you're still very young right now.

facts.quizQuestion:
宿題を{___}終わっていない。
(I [still] haven't finished my homework.)
facts.explanation: 未だ (yet / as yet / so far/ not yet) — 宿題を未だ終わっていない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes an unfinished task — the homework was supposed to be done, but it still isn't, even now.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}の機転で、災難を逃れることができた。
(Due to my [instinctive] quick wits, I was able to escape the calamity.)
facts.explanation: とっさに (To reflexively react; an unconscious movement.) — とっさの機転で、災難を逃れることができた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The quick thinking happened automatically — there was no time to plan; the speaker's wits kicked in on their own.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の反応は{___}出たものだ。
(His response is [automatic].)
facts.explanation: とっさに (To reflexively react; an unconscious movement.) — 彼の反応はとっさに出たものだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: His response was purely instinctual — it didn't come from deliberate thought, it just came out automatically.

facts.quizQuestion:
何よりも、満員電車に乗ら{___}のはうれしい。
(Above anything, employees are happy for [not having to] ride on overcrowded trains.)
facts.explanation: なくてもすむ (Don't have to 〜　(and are happy because of it)) — 何よりも、満員電車に乗らなくてもすむのはうれしい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Employees are happy because they don't have to ride crowded trains — なくてもすむ means they can get by, or manage fine, without having to do it.

facts.quizQuestion:
今度の試験は易しいから、あまり勉強し{___}。
(The upcoming test should be easy, so I think I can get by [without studying much].)
facts.explanation: なくてもすむ (Don't have to 〜　(and are happy because of it)) — 今度の試験は易しいから、あまり勉強しなくてもすむ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The easy test means you can manage without studying much — なくてもすむ shows you can get through the situation without needing to do that action.

facts.quizQuestion:
うちの息子{___}、全然勉強しないで、ゲームばっかりしてるのよ。
([Speaking of] my son, he is always playing games without studying.)
facts.explanation: ときたら (when it comes to~ (neg. sense)) — うちの息子ときたら、全然勉強しないで、ゲームばっかりしてるのよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is raising my son as a (negative) topic — ときたら introduces a topic with a critical or exasperated tone, signaling a complaint is coming.

facts.quizQuestion:
私{___}、料理はまったくだめなのよ。
(I really can't cook [at all].)
facts.explanation: ときたら (when it comes to~ (neg. sense)) — 私ときたら、料理はまったくだめなのよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is bringing herself up as a topic to self-criticize — ときたら singles out the subject with a negative or self-deprecating framing.

facts.quizQuestion:
それは母体に良く、{___}お腹の赤ちゃんにも良い。
(It is good for the mother's body, [which in turn] is good for her coming baby.)
facts.explanation: ひいては (which in turn) — それは母体に良く、ひいてはお腹の赤ちゃんにも良い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: What's good for the mother's body flows through and benefits the baby too — ひいては shows how one effect extends to a downstream result.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}国際化に繋がるのだ ろうか?
(Will this [in turn] lead to internationalization?)
facts.explanation: ひいては (which in turn) — ひいては国際化に繋がるのだ ろうか?
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The first effect might lead further to internationalization — ひいては connects the immediate outcome to its broader, downstream consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
今年の収支は赤字である{___}、来年はなんとか黒字にしよう。
(Our balance sheet is in the red this year; [consequently], let's try to somehow come out on top next year.)
facts.explanation: からして (Since; because; Consequently) — 今年の収支は赤字であるからして、来年はなんとか黒字にしよう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Takes a negative current situation as the reason to act — being in the red this year is the direct motivation to fix things next year.

facts.quizQuestion:
そういう噂{___}、あの二人は近々結婚するだろう。
([Since] that sort of rumor is going around, consequently, it's likely that those two will get married soon.)
facts.explanation: からして (Since; because; Consequently) — そういう噂からして、あの二人は近々結婚するだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Uses a circulating rumor as the logical basis for drawing a conclusion — the gossip is what makes the prediction reasonable.

facts.quizQuestion:
iPadが欲しい。ミニだ{___}エアーだ{___}欲しい。
(I want an iPad. [Doesn't matter] Mini or Air, I want it.)
facts.explanation: であろうが (A or B doesn't matter, at least I want C) — iPadが欲しい。ミニだろうがエアーだろうが欲しい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Two iPad models are presented as options — Mini or Air — but neither matters because both outcomes lead to the same result: wanting it.

facts.quizQuestion:
住みやすか{___}住みにくか{___}、大事なのは家賃が低いことです
([Doesn't matter] easy or hard to live in, what matters is cheap rent.)
facts.explanation: であろうが (A or B doesn't matter, at least I want C) — 住みやすかろうが住みにくかろうが、大事なのは家賃が低いことです
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: Comfortable or not, the speaker doesn't care either way. The only thing that actually matters is cheap rent.

facts.quizQuestion:
２人っきりでデート・・{___}、ＯＦＦ会だと思ったけど、そこまで甘くもないよな。
(So I thought my date [er rather] offline-meeting was going to be just me and her but things aren't that easy.)
facts.explanation: もとい (is wrong, rather) — ２人っきりでデート・・もとい、ＯＦＦ会だと思ったけど、そこまで甘くもないよな。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker uses もとい to catch and correct themselves mid-sentence — they said 'date' but immediately stepped back to the less romantic word 'offline meeting.'

facts.correctAnswer: と伝えていただけませんか
facts.quizQuestion:
彼におめでとう{___}。
([Please tell him] congratulations from me.)
facts.explanation: と伝えていただけませんか (Politely asking someone to convey a message.) — 彼におめでとうと伝えていただけませんか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.targetLanguageWord: と伝えていただけませんか
facts.reading: につたえていたでけませんか
facts.pronunciation: につたえていたでけませんか
facts.grammarNote: The speaker wants someone else to relay a message on their behalf. と伝えていただけませんか is the set phrase for this indirect, polite request.

facts.quizQuestion:
会社のメールに山田さん{___}のレポートがあります。
(There is a report [from] Mr. Yamada in the company's e-mail.)
facts.explanation: より (from) — 会社のメールに山田さんよりのレポートがあります。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Marks the sender as the origin of the report — より here indicates the source the email came from, not a comparison.

facts.quizQuestion:
夜が{___}長くなる.
(The nights are [gradually] gaining on the day.)
facts.explanation: 次第に (Gradually, little by little) — 夜が次第に長くなる.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Describes a slow, ongoing change — the nights aren't suddenly long, they're getting longer bit by bit over time.

facts.quizQuestion:
被疑者が検察官の、自白をすれば起訴猶予する{___}のことばを信じた。
(The suspect believed in the prosecutor's words [that] the prosecution would be delayed if he confessed.)
facts.explanation: 旨 (concerning ... , regarding ... , that ...) — 被疑者が検察官の、自白をすれば起訴猶予する旨のことばを信じた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: 旨 quotes the substance of what the prosecutor said — not the exact words, but the key point that the suspect believed and acted on.

facts.quizQuestion:
Wikipediaはブリタニカ{___}正確だ。
(Wikipedia is [as accurate as] Britannica.)
facts.explanation: 並みに (As...as, to the same degree as) — Wikipediaはブリタニカ並みに正確だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: Sets Wikipedia on equal footing with Britannica in terms of accuracy — matching two things to the same standard is what this pattern does.

facts.quizQuestion:
たばこを止める{___}しました。
(I [have decided] to quit smoking.)
facts.explanation: ことにする ((I/you/he/she/we/they) has/have decided that...) — たばこを止めることにしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: Quitting smoking is the decision that was made. ことにする marks this as a personal, conscious choice the speaker has committed to.

facts.quizQuestion:
Q. ピクニックは どこが　いいですか？　A. どこ{___} いいです。
(Q. Where is the best spot for a picnic? A. [Anywhere] will do.)
facts.explanation: Q~でも (Question word + demo = Question word + ever. (Whenever, wherever, whatever, whoever)) — Q. ピクニックは どこが　いいですか？　A. どこでも いいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: どこでも means any place will work — the question asked where, and the answer is 'wherever, it doesn't matter.'

facts.quizQuestion:
能力{___}
([lack of] ability.)
facts.explanation: のなさ (lack of something) — 能力のなさ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Lack of ability is expressed by nominalizing 'no ability' into a noun phrase — のなさ turns an adjective or verb into a noun meaning 'the absence of X.'

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな大きなケーキをよくもひとりで食べられる{___}
([How] could he eat a cake that big all by himself?)
facts.explanation: よくも〜ものだ (How the hell is it possible, that ...?) — こんな大きなケーキをよくもひとりで食べられるものだ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is amazed — eating a whole cake that big, alone, seems almost impossible. よくも～ものだ expresses that disbelief and mild outrage.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}の果てに彼は刑務所行きになった。
([In the end] he landed up in jail.)
facts.explanation: 挙げ句 (in the end, after all) — 挙げ句の果てに彼は刑務所行きになった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Shows a long downward spiral reaching its worst outcome — after everything he went through, prison was the final result.

facts.quizQuestion:
散々考えた{___}その計画を実行にうつした
([After] hard thinking, I carried out the plan.)
facts.explanation: 挙げ句 (in the end, after all) — 散々考えた挙げ句その計画を実行にうつした
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Shows a prolonged decision process finally reaching action — after all that hard thinking, he committed to the plan.

synonymGroups.reason: から and ので are often interchangeable for 'because', but ので is softer and more objective; から is more direct and subjective
らしい, よう, みたい, そう(hearsay), の様 all express 'seems/apparently' but differ in formality, information source, and directness of inference
なければならない, せねばならない, なければなりません are near-synonyms for obligation, differing mainly in formality and register
ことにする (1629 and 1769 are the same pattern, different IDs — volitional decision) vs ことになる (external/natural result that has been decided)
しか (requires negative verb), さえ (even/only — extremity marker), だけ (just/only — neutral), ばかり (nothing but/just did), こそ (emphasis on this one thing)
